Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c5, r6c7 (also r6c3)
      row 8: r8c5, r8c8 (also r8c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 9: r9c4, r9c9 (also r9c3)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 9), r8c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4} in box 8, {r8c8, r7c7, r9c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r9c4 guards oddagon {r9c4, r8c5, [r6c7=r8c8], r6c3, r9c3} (source r8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r9c4) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, r7c6}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c5} and {r6c5, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
        row 8 {[r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí [r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c4]‚âÝX
        box 8 {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
        r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8]}: r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r8c3=r9c4], r9c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c7=r8c8]}: r5c8, [r6c7=r8c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
        r4c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {[r8c3=r9c4], r9c3, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8]}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8]‚âÝX
        box 8 {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5‚âÝX
        r6c5=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {[r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c5, r6c7 (also r6c3)

      row 9: r9c4, r9c9 (also r9c3)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 9), r8c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4} in box 8, {r8c8, r7c7, r9c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 9), r8c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 9, 7: {r8c5, r9c4, r7c6} in box 8, {r8c8, r9c9, r7c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r8c8] and [r6c7=r8c8]).

          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_parity] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3‚Äîr9c3, r8c5‚Äîr9c4, r8c8‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r7c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r5c6 guards oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, r6c7, r7c7, r7c6} (source r6c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r6c3], r7c7, r9c9, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r6c3], r9c9} and {r7c7, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c3]) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3], r6c5, r6c7}: [r5c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c4‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3}: [r5c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c7, r8c8‚âÝX
        [r5c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r6c3], r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
        r7c7=X ‚Üí r6c7, r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3], r6c5, r6c7}: [r5c6=r6c3], r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
        r6c5=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c5, r6c7 (also r6c3)
      row 8: r8c5, r8c8 (also r8c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 9: r9c4, r9c9 (also r9c3)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 9), r8c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4} in box 8, {r8c8, r7c7, r9c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r6c7=r8c8], r6c3, r8c3, r9c3} (spine r6c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color([r6c7=r8c8]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], r8c5, r9c4, r7c6} (spine r8c5‚Äîr9c4).
      ‚Üí color([r6c7=r8c8=r9c3]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c6=r8c8=r9c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, r9c4, r9c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c5, r6c7 (also r6c3)
      row 8: r8c5, r8c8 (also r8c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 9: r9c4, r9c9 (also r9c3)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 9), r8c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4} in box 8, {r8c8, r7c7, r9c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r6c7=r8c8], r6c3, r8c3, r9c3} (spine r6c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color([r6c7=r8c8]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c3, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], r8c5, r9c4} (spine [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3]‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, r7c6}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c5} and {r6c5, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
        row 8 {[r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4]‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4], r9c9}: [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4]‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4]}: [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        box 8 {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], r7c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
        r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3]}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3]‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c4]}: r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8=r9c3]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r8c3=r9c4]=X
        box 8 {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c4], r8c5}: r7c6=X and [r8c3=r9c4]=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.

      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r7c9) = X

        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

      Case 2: color(r7c3) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 9 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r9c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí r7c9, r8c8‚âÝX
        r9c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r6c7}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
        r6c5=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c3, r6c7 (also r6c5)
      row 8: r8c3, r8c8 (also r8c5)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 9: r9c2, r9c7 (also r9c6)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c3, r7c3, r9c2} in box 7, {r8c8, r7c9, r9c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c7‚Üîr9c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8] (also r6c5)
      row 8: r8c3, [r6c7=r8c8] (also r8c5)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 9: r9c2, r9c7 (also r9c6)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c3, r7c3, r9c2} in box 7, {r8c8, r7c9, r9c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r8c8] and [r6c7=r8c8]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c7‚Üîr9c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c7=r8c8]) = color([r6c7=r8c8]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c3, r6c7 (also r6c5)
      row 8: r8c3, r8c8 (also r8c5)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 9: r9c2, r9c7 (also r9c6)
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c3, r7c3, r9c2} in box 7, {r8c8, r7c9, r9c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c7‚Üîr9c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r9c7 guards oddagon {r9c7, [r6c7=r8c8], r6c3, r7c3, r9c2} (source r8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r9c7) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c5} and {r6c5, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
        row 8 {[r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c7], r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí [r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c7]‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r6c7=r8c8], r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7]}: [r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c7]‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
        r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c5, r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8]}: r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        row 9 {[r8c3=r9c7], r9c2, r9c6}: [r8c3=r9c7], r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c7=r8c8]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c7=r8c8]}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8]‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r8c3=r9c7], r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r8c3=r9c7], r9c2‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, [r8c3=r9c7]}: r6c3, [r8c3=r9c7]‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
        box 9 {[r6c7=r8c8], r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7]}: [r6c7=r8c8], [r8c3=r9c7]‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
        r6c5=X ‚Üí r5c6, r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c3=X and r7c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r4c9 guards oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r8c5, r8c8, r7c9} (source [r6c5=r9c7]).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color([r6c5=r9c7]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r6c5=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c7).

      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r7c9, r7c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r7c9} and {[r6c5=r9c7], r7c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r6c7}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí [r6c5=r9c7], r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r9c6}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r6c5=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
        [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r6c5=r9c7]) = color(r7c3) = X
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r6c7}: [r6c5=r9c7]=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r9c6}: [r6c5=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r6c5=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r6c5=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r7c9, r8c8‚âÝX
        [r6c5=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c9, r9c7, r9c2}:

              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r7c3‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c6=r7c5], r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c5]=X
              [r5c6=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c6=r7c5], r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c5]=X
              [r5c6=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r5c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
              r6c3=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r7c5). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6}
            Bridges: r7c5‚Äîr7c3 (edges r5c6‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr9c2), r6c7‚Äîr9c7 (edges r6c5‚Äîr6c3 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr9c6), r7c3‚Äîr7c5 (edges r6c3‚Äîr8c3 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr5c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c7, r9c6} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr9c7).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r8c3 guards oddagon {r8c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r7c5, r8c4} (source [r6c7=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color([r6c7=r9c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r8c3=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c6, r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c7, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r6c5=r9c7], r9c6, [r6c3=r9c2]} (spine [r6c5=r9c7]‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r8c9, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r8c9} and {[r6c5=r9c7], r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r6c7}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí [r6c5=r9c7], r6c7‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c4‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r9c6}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
        box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c5=X
        [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r8c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r6c5=r9c7]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r6c7}: [r6c5=r9c7]=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r6c7‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c7], r9c6}: [r6c5=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
        box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c5=X
        [r6c5=r9c7]=X and r7c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r8c6} (spine r5c6‚Äî[r5c8=r6c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c7, [r4c9=r6c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r6c6], r9c8} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c6], [r4c9=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c7=r9c8], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c7=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c6]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c6=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c7=r9c8], r9c3} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c7=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c6=r7c9]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c5=r8c6], [r5c8=r6c6=r7c9=r9c3], r8c3, r8c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (SET+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c8} = {r4c9, r8c3}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c8, r8c6} = {r6c6, r7c9}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Trivalue oddagon:
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7} [even]
      box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}
        ‚Üí via cols {7, 8, 9} [odd]
      box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7} [even]
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {3, 6, 7} [even]
      Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (SET+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r8c9} = {r4c9, r9c6}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c8, r9c3} = {r6c6, r7c8}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Trivalue oddagon:
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7} [odd]
      box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7, 8, 9} [odd]
      box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7} [odd]
      row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {3, 6, 7} [even]
      Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r8c9, r8c5} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], r5c5, r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c5]‚Äîr5c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r9c7, r7c8}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, r9c7} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c6=r9c5], r9c2, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí [r6c6=r9c5], r9c2‚âÝX
        col 5 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], r5c5, [r6c6=r9c5]}: r5c5, [r6c6=r9c5]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c3=r8c5]=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r6c6=r9c5]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], [r6c6=r9c5], r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], [r6c6=r9c5]‚âÝX
        col 5 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], r5c5, [r6c6=r9c5]}: [r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], [r6c6=r9c5]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r6c6=r9c5]}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c7‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c9}: [r4c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        row 9 {[r6c6=r9c5], r9c2, r9c7}: [r6c6=r9c5], r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (SET+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3‚Äîr9c2, r8c5‚Äîr9c5, r8c9‚Äîr9c7}:
      Satellites r5c5 (rung 2), r7c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.

      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr8c9, r5c8‚Äîr7c8, r6c7‚Äîr9c7}:

  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c8, r5c5, r8c3}:

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r5c5) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], r6c6, r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
        col 5 {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
        [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r5c5=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c9‚âÝX

        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c3], r6c7‚âÝX
        r5c8=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c5=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c6=r8c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c8], [r5c5=r8c6], [r5c8=r6c6=r8c3=r9c9], r9c5} (spine [r5c5=r8c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c6=r8c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c8]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r8c8=r9c5], [r5c8=r6c6=r8c3=r9c9], [r6c7=r9c3], r7c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_guardian] (ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3‚Äîr9c3, r8c6‚Äîr9c5, r8c8‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r7c7, r9c9, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c9} and {r7c7, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c5‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c7, r8c8‚âÝX
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c6=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
        r7c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c6=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6=X and r8c6=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.

      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r9c3 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r8c6, r9c5, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+hexagon+guardian) ===

      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c5‚Äîr9c5, [r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr9c3}:
      Satellites r8c5 (rung 1), r8c7 (rung 2), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c5, r5c8, r6c7, r6c3, r9c3, r9c5}
      Bridges: [r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr6c6 (edges r5c5‚Äîr5c8 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr9c3), r4c9‚Äîr9c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr9c5), r8c7‚Äîr8c5 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c3 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr5c5).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c9, r9c5} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5]‚Äîr5c5).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c5], r6c7, r8c7, r7c8, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===

      ‚Üí color(r9c9) = color([r5c8=r6c6]). Identify.

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr9c9, [r5c8=r6c6]‚Äîr7c8, r6c7‚Äîr8c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c6], r7c6, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r4c9=r6c3], r7c6} and {[r5c8=r6c6], r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7‚âÝX

        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c4‚âÝX
        [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r5c8=r6c6]) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r5c8=r6c6]}: [r5c8=r6c6]=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c6]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c3], r6c7‚âÝX

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r7c6} (spine r5c6‚Äî[r5c8=r6c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c8=r6c6], r7c8} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c9, r9c4} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  6.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7 (also [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4])
      row 9: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], r9c9 (also r9c3)
      row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c6=r7c8]
      row 8: r8c5, r8c7
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c8, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r9c9).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  7.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r5c8=r6c6], [r6c7=r9c9] (also [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4])
      row 9: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c4], [r6c7=r9c9] (also r9c3)
      row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c6=r7c8]
      row 8: r8c5, r8c7
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c8, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r9c9] and [r6c7=r9c9]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.

          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c7=r9c9]) = color([r6c7=r9c9]). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r7c8 guards oddagon {r7c8, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r7c6} (source r5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r7c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr7c6, r8c7‚Äîr8c5, r9c9‚Äîr9c4}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr9c3, r7c6‚Äîr9c4}:
      Satellites r8c7 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r8c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r8c7, r9c9, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c9} and {r8c7, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c4‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c8], r8c7‚âÝX
        r6c3=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {[r5c6=r7c8], r6c6, r7c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c7) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c8]‚âÝX
        r8c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7, r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r7c8 guards oddagon {r7c8, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r7c6} (source r5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r7c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr7c6, r8c7‚Äîr8c4, r9c9‚Äîr9c5}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c5‚Äîr9c5, [r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr9c3}:
      Satellites r8c7 (rung 2), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r8c7, r9c9, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c9} and {r8c7, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c5‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c8], r8c7‚âÝX
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {[r5c6=r7c8], r6c6, r7c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
        r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c7) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c8]‚âÝX
        r8c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7, r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r7c8 guards oddagon {r7c8, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r7c6} (source r5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r7c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr7c6, r8c7‚Äîr8c4, r9c9‚Äîr9c4}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r5c6=r7c8]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr9c3, r7c6‚Äîr9c4}:
      Satellites r8c7 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r8c4 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r8c7, r9c9, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c9} and {r8c7, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c4‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c8], r8c7‚âÝX
        r6c3=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {[r5c6=r7c8], r6c6, r7c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6}: [r5c6=r7c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c7) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c6=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r7c8]‚âÝX
        r8c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7, r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c5, r7c5, r9c5, r8c4} (spine r7c5‚Äîr9c5).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  4.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr9c9, [r5c8=r6c6]‚Äîr8c8, r6c7‚Äîr7c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), [r5c5=r8c4] (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c6], [r5c5=r8c4], r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c5=r8c4]} and {[r5c8=r6c6], r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color([r5c5=r8c4]) = X
        row 5 {[r5c5=r8c4], r5c6, [r5c8=r6c6]}: [r5c5=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c6, [r5c8=r6c6]‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX

        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r5c8=r6c6]) = color(r9c3) = X

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r9c8 guards oddagon {r9c8, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r9c6} (source r5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r9c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr7c9, r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r9c8], r6c7‚Äîr8c7}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r5c5 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c8, r5c5, r7c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c5} and {r5c8, r7c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r5c5) = X

        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], r6c6, r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r5c6=r9c8], r6c6, r9c6}: [r5c6=r9c8], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
        [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r7c3, r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c2=X
        box 9 {[r5c6=r9c8], r7c9, r8c7}: [r5c6=r9c8], r7c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
        r8c2=X and r8c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r7c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r9c8], r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, [r5c6=r9c8]‚âÝX

        row 9 {[r5c6=r9c8], r9c2, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

  5.  Gradient chain (with identification):

        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color([r5c6=r9c8]). Identify.
  6.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+ladder+xwing+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

      ‚Üí color(r9c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.

      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr7c9, r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r9c8], r6c7‚Äîr8c7}:

      Diagonals: {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c5} and {r5c8, r7c3} (non-adjacent).

        r6c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c3=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {[r5c6=r9c8], r9c1, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c4=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c4=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c7, r9c8‚âÝX
        r6c4=X ‚Üí r6c3, r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c4, r8c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c4]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c8} (spine r9c1‚Äîr9c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c7, [r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c4], [r8c3=r9c8]} (spine [r4c9=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r8c3=r9c8]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c4], [r4c9=r6c3], [r6c7=r8c3=r9c8], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c3]‚Äî[r6c7=r8c3=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c8=r6c4=r7c9], [r6c7=r8c3=r9c8], r8c4, r8c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (SET+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c8} = {r4c9, r8c3}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c8, r8c4} = {r6c4, r7c9}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Trivalue oddagon:
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7} [even]
      box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}
        ‚Üí via cols {7, 8, 9} [odd]
      box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {7} [even]
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}
        ‚Üí via cols {3, 4, 7} [even]
      Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c4]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r8c9, r8c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c4], [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], r5c6, r9c6} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6], r6c7} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6], r5c5} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r5c5). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6], r9c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c7], [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c7], [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6], r7c8} (spine [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c7]) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  9.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c7], [r5c6=r6c7=r7c8], [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6]}
      row 6 {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c7], [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6], [r5c6=r6c7=r7c8]}
      row 8 {r8c3, [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c7], r8c9}
      row 9 {r9c2, [r5c8=r6c4=r9c6], [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c7]}
      Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, box); row 6‚Üîrow 8 (col, box, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, box)
      ‚Üí same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r8c9, r8c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).

  3.  Diamond {r6c4, [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], r5c6, r9c6} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r6c4) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r9c7, r7c8}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, r9c7} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).

      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r9c7) = X

        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r6c4=r9c6]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X

        col 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], r5c6, [r6c4=r9c6]}: [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r6c4=r9c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r6c4=r9c6]}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX

        r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3‚Äîr9c2, r8c6‚Äîr9c6, r8c9‚Äîr9c7}:

      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.

        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], r6c4, r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX

      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r8c3) = X

        Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c4]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r8c9, r8c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c4], r7c8, r9c7}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r6c7], r7c8} and {[r5c8=r6c4], r9c7} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c7]) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c4]}: [r5c6=r6c7]=X ‚Üí r5c5, [r5c8=r6c4]‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c4]}: [r5c6=r6c7]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7], r9c6}: [r5c6=r6c7]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c7‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c9}: [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c4]}: [r5c8=r6c4]=X ‚Üí r5c5, [r5c6=r6c7]‚âÝX

        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c6‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7], r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r7c7) = X

        row 6 {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3, r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        row 9 {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r9c4, r9c8}: [r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X

        Contradiction.

        col 3 {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c3], r6c3, r8c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r9c4 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c4]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r8c3, r8c9, r8c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c7], [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c8=r6c4], r9c4} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c7]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4], [r5c8=r6c4], r5c5} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r5c5). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4], [r5c8=r6c4], r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c4], [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c8], [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4], r7c7} (spine [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c8]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c7=r9c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  9.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c8], [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4], [r5c8=r6c4=r7c7]}
      row 6 {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c8], [r5c8=r6c4=r7c7], [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4]}
      row 8 {r8c3, [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c8], r8c9}
      row 9 {r9c2, [r5c6=r6c7=r9c4], [r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6=r9c8]}
      Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 8 (box, col, box); row 6‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
      ‚Üí same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r7c7:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r7c7], r6c4, r9c4, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r6c3=r7c7], r9c4} and {r6c4, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r6c3=r7c7]) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r7c7], r6c4, r6c7}: [r6c3=r7c7]=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c4=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c3=r7c7], r8c9, r9c8}: [r6c3=r7c7]=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX
              [r6c3=r7c7]=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c4=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c4) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r7c7], r6c4, r6c7}: r6c4=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r7c7], r6c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c3=r7c7], r8c9, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX
              r9c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c7). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r7c7, r8c9, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r8c9) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c8‚âÝX
              r8c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX
              r7c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c4=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c2, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
              r6c4=X and r9c4=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.

      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r9c2). Identify.

      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color([r5c8=r6c4=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c4=r8c9=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c7, r7c7, r9c8, r9c4, r8c6, r8c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5‚Äîr9c5).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr9c9, r5c8‚Äîr7c8, r6c7‚Äîr8c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), [r5c5=r7c6] (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c3], r5c8, [r5c5=r7c6], r9c3}:

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]) = X
        row 5 {[r5c5=r7c6], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX

        [r5c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c4]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5‚Äîr9c5).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c8=r6c4], r7c8} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c9, r9c5} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  6.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7 (also [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5])
      row 9: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5], r9c9 (also r9c3)
      row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c6=r7c8]
      row 8: r8c5, r8c7
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c8, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c4‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r9c9).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c4‚Üîr7c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c4‚Üîr9c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  7.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r5c8=r6c4], [r6c7=r9c9] (also [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5])
      row 9: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c5], [r6c7=r9c9] (also r9c3)
      row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c6=r7c8]
      row 8: r8c5, r8c7
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c8, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c4‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r9c9] and [r6c7=r9c9]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c4‚Üîr7c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c4‚Üîr9c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c7=r9c9]) = color([r6c7=r9c9]). Identify.

      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r9c5]) = color([r5c6=r7c8]). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c5, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r5c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c5=r9c3], r5c8, r6c7, r6c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c5=r9c3], r6c7} and {r5c8, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c5=r9c3]) = color(r6c7) = X
              row 5 {[r5c5=r9c3], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c5=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r5c5=r9c3], r9c5, r9c9}: [r5c5=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 5 {[r5c5=r9c3], r8c5, r9c5}: [r5c5=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r6c7=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {[r5c5=r9c3], r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí [r5c5=r9c3], r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r5c5=r9c3], r5c6, r6c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c5) ‚âÝ color(r9c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c5, r6c4, r6c3, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c5, r6c3} and {r6c4, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c5) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX
              col 5 {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c4) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX

              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        row 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c4]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c3], r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
        [r5c8=r6c4]=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c4]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c5, [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r6c4], r8c8} (spine [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c9, r9c6} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  6.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r5c8=r6c4], r6c7 (also [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6])
      row 9: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6], r9c9 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c5=r8c8]
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c6, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r9c9).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c4‚Üîr8c4 (col 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  7.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r5c8=r6c4], [r6c7=r9c9] (also [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6])
      row 9: [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6], [r6c7=r9c9] (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c5=r8c8]
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c6, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r9c9] and [r6c7=r9c9]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c7‚Üîr7c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c4‚Üîr8c4 (col 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c7=r9c9]) = color([r6c7=r9c9]). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c4], [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6], [r5c6=r8c4], r7c6} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6]‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  9.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c4], [r5c5=r8c8], [r5c8=r6c4=r7c6], r7c7} (spine [r5c5=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4=r7c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  10.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c6], [r5c6=r7c7=r8c4], [r5c8=r6c4=r7c6], [r5c5=r8c8]} (spine [r5c6=r7c7=r8c4]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c4=r7c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r9c6]) = color([r5c5=r8c8]). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r9c3], r5c8, r6c7, r6c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c6=r9c3], r6c7} and {r5c8, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c6=r9c3]) = color(r6c7) = X
              row 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r9c3], r5c8}: [r5c6=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r5c6=r9c3], r9c6, r9c9}: [r5c6=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c6=r9c3], r7c6, r9c6}: [r5c6=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
              r6c7=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r9c3], r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, [r5c6=r9c3]‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 5 {r5c5, [r5c6=r9c3], r6c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c4, r6c3, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c3} and {r6c4, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c4) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
              r6c4=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3], r9c3, r9c9, r9c6} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6], r5c6, r7c6} (spine [r4c9=r6c3=r9c6]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c6], r6c7, r7c7, r8c8, r8c4} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r7c6‚Äîr7c7, r8c4‚Äîr8c8, r9c6‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites [r5c8=r6c4] (rung 2), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Guardian: r9c9 guards oddagon {r9c9, r4c9, r6c7, r6c3, r9c3} (source [r5c8=r6c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r9c9) = color([r5c8=r6c4]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c4=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c7, r7c7, r8c8, r8c4, r9c6, r9c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r7c7:
