        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí r7c9, r8c8‚âÝX
        r9c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c4=X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX
        r6c4=X and r8c4=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r9c2) = X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c3, r6c7 (also r6c4)
      row 8: r8c3, r8c8 (also r8c4)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 9: r9c2, r9c7
      Rows 8, 7, 9: {r8c3, r7c3, r9c2} in box 7, {r8c8, r7c9, r9c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c4‚Üîr8c4 (col 4) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c7‚Üîr9c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c3, [r6c7=r8c8] (also r6c4)

      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 9: r9c2, r9c7

      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c4‚Üîr8c4 (col 4) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r8c8] and [r6c7=r8c8]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 6}.
      Remainder: {r6c3} = {r4c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r7c3‚Äîr7c9, r8c2‚Äîr8c7, r9c2‚Äîr9c8}:
      Satellites [r4c9=r6c3] (rung 1), r9c4 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Guardian: r9c8 guards oddagon {r9c8, r5c8, r6c7, r6c4, r9c4} (source [r4c9=r6c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r9c8) = color([r4c9=r6c3]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r6c3=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c4, r6c7, r8c7, r7c9, r7c3, r9c2, r9c4} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  5.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r4c9=r6c3], [r6c7=r9c8] (also [r5c8=r6c4=r9c1])
      row 9: [r5c8=r6c4=r9c1], [r6c7=r9c8] (also r9c4)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 8: r8c2, r8c7
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c8, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4), r6c4‚Üîr9c4 (col 4) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c7=r9c8] and [r6c7=r9c8]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.

        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c7=r9c8]) = color([r6c7=r9c8]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c4=r9c1], [r4c9=r6c3], r7c3, r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c3]‚Äîr7c3).

      Satellites [r4c9=r6c3] (rung 1), r9c4 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Guardian: r9c8 guards oddagon {r9c8, r5c8, r6c7, r6c4, r9c4} (source [r4c9=r6c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r9c8) = color([r4c9=r6c3]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r6c3=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c4, r6c7, r8c7, r7c9, r7c3, r9c1, r9c4} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

          r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c8, r9c2, r9c7} = {r4c9, r7c9, r8c3}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub r8c8 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r6c6=r8c5], r6c8, r9c7, r7c9, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
      Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c7‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
        r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r6c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
        r5c3=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c9)
      row 6: r6c3, r6c6 (also [r6c8=r7c9])
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5 (also r8c7)
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c7 (col 7) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r8c5).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.

          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c7 (col 7) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c9 and r8c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also [r5c9=r8c7])
      row 6: r6c3, [r6c6=r8c5] (also [r6c8=r7c9])

        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c9=r8c7] and [r5c9=r8c7]).

      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r6c6=r8c5], [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3=r9c6], r6c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c6=r8c5]‚Äî[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c7, r8c3, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c7=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r7c9, r9c7, r9c2, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        r5c3=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+SET+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4} = {r4c9, r7c3, r8c6}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Diamond {r8c6, r7c3, r8c2, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], [r8c6=r9c2]} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color([r8c6=r9c2]). Identify.
  5.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c3, r6c6 (also [r6c8=r9c9])
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also [r5c9=r8c6=r9c2])
      row 9: [r5c9=r8c6=r9c2], r9c4 (also [r6c8=r9c9])
      row 7: r7c3, r7c5
      row 8: r8c2, [r5c9=r8c6=r9c2]
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c4, r7c5, r8c6} in box 8 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 9: r5c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4), r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c6 and r9c4).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r9c4], [r5c9=r8c6=r9c2], r6c6, [r6c8=r9c9]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c6, r7c3, r8c2, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c6=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r7c3, r7c5, r9c4, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r8c6, r8c2}:
      Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c6} and {r7c5, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}
      Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6‚Üîrow 8 (col, col, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
      ‚Üí same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_parity] (ladder+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c8, r9c8, r7c9}:
            Diagonals: {r4c9, r9c8} and {r6c8, r7c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c9, r7c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r6c3=r9c2], r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c5=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r7c9) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r6c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c7, r9c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}: [r6c3=r9c2], r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: r5c3=X and r8c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c2). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r7c9, r9c8, r9c2, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c8‚Äîr9c2), r4c9‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr7c9 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr6c3), r8c7‚Äîr8c3 (edges r7c9‚Äîr9c8 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_parity] (ladder+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:

        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9]}: [r6c8=r7c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c7}: [r6c8=r7c9]=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX
        r5c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r8c4]) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r8c4], [r6c8=r7c9]}: [r6c5=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9]‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r6c5=r8c4], r8c3, r8c8}: [r6c5=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c9)
      row 6: r6c3, r6c5 (also [r6c8=r7c9])
      row 8: r8c3, r8c4 (also r8c8)
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r8c4).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c5) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c9)
      row 6: r6c3, [r6c5=r8c4] (also [r6c8=r7c9])
      row 8: r8c3, [r6c5=r8c4] (also r8c8)
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c9 and r8c8).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also [r5c9=r8c8])
      row 6: r6c3, [r6c5=r8c4] (also [r6c8=r7c9])
      row 8: r8c3, [r6c5=r8c4] (also [r5c9=r8c8])
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c9=r8c8] and [r5c9=r8c8]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c9=r8c8]) = color([r5c9=r8c8]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c9], r6c3, [r6c5=r8c4], r8c3} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c5=r8c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], [r6c5=r8c4]} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r8c4]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c7, r9c6} (spine r9c2‚Äîr9c7).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r6c5=r8c4], [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3=r9c6], r6c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c5=r8c4]‚Äî[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (ladder+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c3, r8c4, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c4} and {r8c3, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c8}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c8}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c2). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r7c9, r9c7, r9c2, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c2), r4c9‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr7c9 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr6c3), r8c8‚Äîr8c3 (edges r7c9‚Äîr9c7 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      row 6: r6c3, [r6c5=r8c4] (also [r6c8=r7c9])
      row 8: r8c3, [r6c5=r8c4] (also [r5c9=r8c7])
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c8)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c4‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c8 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c9=r8c7] and [r5c9=r8c7]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c9=r8c7]) = color([r5c9=r8c7]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c9], r6c3, [r6c5=r8c4], r8c3} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c5=r8c4]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], [r6c5=r8c4]} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r8c4]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c8, r9c6} (spine r9c2‚Äîr9c8).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r6c5=r8c4], [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3=r9c6], r6c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c5=r8c4]‚Äî[r6c8=r7c9=r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        2.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c4]}
            row 8 {r8c3, [r6c8=r8c4], r8c7}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}
            Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6‚Üîrow 8 (col, box, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
            ‚Üí same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c4, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c4, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r8c7, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c4) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r8c7, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c4=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_parity] (ladder+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c3, r8c4, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c4} and {r8c3, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r8c7, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r9c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r8c7, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c2). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r7c9, r9c8, r9c2, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c8‚Äîr9c2), r4c9‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr7c9 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr6c3), r8c7‚Äîr8c3 (edges r7c9‚Äîr9c8 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c3, r8c4, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c4} and {r8c3, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c2). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r7c9, r9c7, r9c2, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c2), r4c9‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr7c9 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr6c3), r8c7‚Äîr8c3 (edges r7c9‚Äîr9c7 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:

      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c4:

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r6c5}:

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r5c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r8c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c6=r8c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c5], r5c3, [r5c9=r8c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c9=r8c3]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r6c3=r8c5], [r5c9=r8c3], [r6c8=r9c9], r8c7}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r8c5, r7c6} (spine r5c6‚Äîr8c5).
            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        8.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r9c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, [r6c8=r9c9]}
            Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6‚Üîrow 8 (col, col, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
            ‚Üí same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, [r6c5=r7c6]}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, [r6c5=r7c6]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6]‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c7, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 8 {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r9c4}: [r6c5=r7c6], r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              r9c4=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r7c6]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r9c4}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c4‚âÝX
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c7, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r7c6, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r7c6} and {r5c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c4‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c7=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX

              r8c3=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)

              r9c4=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r8c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c6=r8c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c3, r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r8c7, r9c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        4.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r8c5, r7c6} (spine r5c6‚Äîr8c5).
            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr8c5 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6]), r4c9‚Äîr6c8 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî [r6c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c3), r9c2‚Äîr8c3 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c4 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        4.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r9c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c8}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, [r6c8=r9c9]}
            Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6‚Üîrow 8 (col, box, col); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, box)
            ‚Üí same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        6.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c6, r8c3, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr8c3).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r5c9, [r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8} (spine [r4c9=r8c4]‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r6c8=r9c9], [r4c9=r8c4], [r5c9=r8c8], r8c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c4]‚Äî[r5c9=r8c8]).
            ‚Üí color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9], r7c6, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c9, r6c8, r8c8, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r5c9, r8c8} and {r6c8, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c4], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r8c4], r8c3‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r8c4], r8c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c4], r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r8c4], r6c5‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r6c3=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: [r6c3=r8c4], r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c4], r8c3, r8c8}: [r6c3=r8c4], r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r8c4], r8c3}: r5c3=X and r8c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, r8c4, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr8c4), r4c9‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr6c3), r9c2‚Äîr8c3 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c4 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r5c9 guards oddagon {r5c9, r5c3, r8c3, r8c8, r6c8} (source [r6c3=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color([r6c3=r8c4]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r6c3=r8c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r5c6, r7c6, r9c4, r9c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, r8c4, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr8c4), r4c9‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr6c3), r9c2‚Äîr8c3 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c4 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        4.  Parity transport:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
            row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r9c9]}
            row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c7}
            row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, [r6c8=r9c9]}
            Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (col, box, box); row 6‚Üîrow 8 (col, box, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, box)
            ‚Üí same permutation parity.
            Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
            4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        6.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c6, r8c3, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr8c3).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r5c9, [r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r9c9], r8c7} (spine [r4c9=r8c4]‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r6c8=r9c9], [r4c9=r8c4], [r5c9=r8c7], r8c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c4]‚Äî[r5c9=r8c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9], r7c6, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              row 9 {[r8c4=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4}: [r8c4=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r5c9=r8c3], r6c3}: r5c3, [r5c9=r8c3]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c3], [r8c4=r9c9]}: [r8c4=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c3], r6c8}: r4c9, [r5c9=r8c3]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, [r8c4=r9c9], r9c4}: [r8c4=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c3=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c9=r8c3]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r8c3]}: [r5c9=r8c3]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 8 {[r5c9=r8c3], [r8c4=r9c9], r8c7}: [r5c9=r8c3]=X ‚Üí [r8c4=r9c9], r8c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r5c9=r8c3], r6c3}: [r5c9=r8c3]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c3], [r8c4=r9c9]}: [r5c9=r8c3]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c3], r6c8}: [r5c9=r8c3]=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, [r8c4=r9c9], r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              [r5c9=r8c3]=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              row 9 {[r8c4=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c7, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r7c6, r8c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              r9c4=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c7, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c9=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c4:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
            Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), [r4c9=r9c4] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r5c3 guards oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r8c3} (source [r4c9=r9c4]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r4c9=r9c4]). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r5c3=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r6c3, r8c3, r8c4, r7c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r8c3, r8c4, r9c4}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r9c9]‚Äîr8c7 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr8c4), r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr8c3 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain:
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9 (also r5c3)
      row 6: r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c5, r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c8
      row 8: r8c4, [r6c8=r8c9]
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5), r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      ‚Üí row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
        r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
        r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
      4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

      row 7: r7c6, r7c8

      Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_parity] (diamond+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c8} and {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r8c4]) = color(r7c8) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c5}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r8c4], r5c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r9c7}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r9c3, r9c5, r8c4}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr9c7 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr9c5), r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr8c4), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.

        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r9c4).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r8c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 5: r5c6, r5c9 (also r5c3)

            Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r9c4).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], [r6c5=r9c4]}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, [r6c8=r8c9]} and {r5c9, [r6c5=r9c4]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r8c9]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX

              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r9c7}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c7‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)

              box 8 {[r6c5=r9c4], r7c6, r8c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r9c4]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX

        4.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8, r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r8c4, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_parity] (diamond+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c8} and {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r8c4]) = color(r7c8) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r8c4], r5c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c8, r9c7}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r9c3, r9c4, r8c4}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr9c7 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr9c4), r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr8c4), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
        r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r8c9‚âÝX
        r6c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
        r7c6=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c9=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
        r8c5=X and r8c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r8c9‚âÝX
        r6c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
        r7c6=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9 (also r5c3)
      row 6: r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c4, r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: r8c5, [r6c8=r8c9]
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r9c4).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9 (also r5c3)
      row 6: [r6c5=r9c4], [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c3)
      row 9: [r6c5=r9c4], r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: r8c5, [r6c8=r8c9]
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c9 and r9c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.

      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r8c5). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_parity] (diamond+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c7} and {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r8c4]) = color(r7c7) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c5}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c7}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r8c4], r5c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c7}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r9c3, r9c5, r8c4}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr9c7 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr9c5), r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr8c4), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c9=r9c8]) = color([r5c9=r9c8]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c3, [r6c5=r9c4], r9c3} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c5=r9c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c8=r9c3], [r6c5=r9c4]} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r9c4]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r6c5=r9c4], r7c6, r8c4} (spine [r5c3=r6c5=r9c4]‚Äîr7c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9 (also r5c3)
      row 6: r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c4, r9c8 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: r8c4, [r6c8=r8c9]
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c8, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c8 (col 8), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r9c4).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3, [r6c5=r9c4]} (spine r6c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r9c4]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c5=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r8c4, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_parity] (diamond+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c7} and {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r8c4]) = color(r7c7) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c8}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c4, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r8c4], r5c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c8}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c8‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c4, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r9c3, r9c4, r8c4}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr9c8 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr9c4), r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr8c4), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°
