            row 9: r9c4, r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c7
            row 8: r8c4, [r6c8=r8c9]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r9c4).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], [r6c5=r9c4]}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, [r6c8=r8c9]} and {r5c9, [r6c5=r9c4]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r8c9]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c4], [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c5=r9c4]‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c7}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c7‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 8 {[r6c5=r9c4], r7c6, r8c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r9c4]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c4], [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c5=r9c4]=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c5=r9c4], r9c3, r9c7}: [r6c5=r9c4]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c9). Add edge.
        4.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8, r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9 (also r5c3)
      row 6: r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c4, r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: r8c4, [r6c8=r8c9]
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c5 and r9c4).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3, [r6c5=r9c4]} (spine r6c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r9c4]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c5=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9], r8c4, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_parity] (diamond+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c4], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c7} and {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r8c4]) = color(r7c7) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: [r4c9=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c7}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c4], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r8c4], r5c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c9=r8c4], r7c6, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r9c7}: [r6c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r9c3, r9c4, r8c4}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr9c7 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr9c4), r5c6‚Äîr7c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr8c4), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9], r8c5} (spine r6c5‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9]).

      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c3=r9c4], r5c6, r7c6} (spine [r5c3=r9c4]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c9=r7c6], [r6c8=r8c9], r7c7, r7c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c8], r5c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c9=r8c8], r5c9, r6c8}: [r4c9=r8c8], r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c8). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r8c2, r8c8}
            Bridges: r9c9‚Äîr9c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr8c2), r5c6‚Äîr8c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r8c2‚Äîr8c8), r6c3‚Äîr6c8 (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r4c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r7c3, [r4c9=r8c8], r8c2, r8c6} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr8c2).
            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, r5c6, [r7c3=r8c6]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color([r7c3=r8c6]). Identify.
        6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c9=r7c3=r8c6], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c8). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r8c8], r8c6} (spine r5c6‚Äî[r5c9=r8c8]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r8c6], [r5c9=r8c8], r8c2} (spine [r5c3=r8c6]‚Äî[r5c9=r8c8]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        10.  Trivalue oddagon:
            row 5 {[r5c3=r8c6], [r5c6=r8c2], [r5c9=r8c8]}
              ‚Üí via cols {9} [odd]
            row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c4}
              ‚Üí via boxes {7} [even]
            col 3 {[r5c3=r8c6], r6c3, r7c3}
              ‚Üí via rows {5} [even]
            col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c8], [r6c8=r9c9]}
              ‚Üí via rows {9} [odd]
            box 7 {[r5c6=r8c2], r7c3, r9c2}
              ‚Üí via cols {3} [odd]
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9]}
              ‚Üí via boxes {5, 6} [even]
            Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_hexagon] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r5c3, r5c6, r8c6, r8c2} (source [r5c9=r8c8]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color([r5c9=r8c8]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r7c3=r8c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c3, r5c3, r5c6, r8c6, r8c2, r9c2, r9c9} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c8). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c2, r8c6, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c5} (spine r6c5‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c9)
      row 6: [r6c3=r9c5], r6c5 (also [r6c8=r9c9])
      row 9: r9c2, [r6c3=r9c5] (also [r6c8=r9c9])
      row 8: r8c2, r8c6 (also r8c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2), r8c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4), r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c8=r9c9] and [r6c8=r9c9]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c5], r7c3, r9c2} (spine [r6c3=r9c5]‚Äîr7c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.

      ‚Üí color([r6c3=r9c5]) = color(r8c2). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c5} (spine r6c5‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c9)
      row 6: [r6c3=r9c5], r6c5 (also [r6c8=r9c9])
      row 9: r9c1, [r6c3=r9c5] (also [r6c8=r9c9])
      row 8: r8c2, r8c6 (also r8c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7), r8c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4), r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c8=r9c9] and [r6c8=r9c9]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c5], r7c3, r9c1} (spine [r6c3=r9c5]‚Äîr7c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c3=r9c5], [r5c3=r9c1], r7c3, r8c2} (spine [r5c3=r9c1]‚Äîr7c3).
      ‚Üí color([r6c3=r9c5]) = color(r8c2). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.

            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color([r7c3=r9c7]). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3=r9c7], r6c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c9=r7c3=r9c7]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        11.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r6c3], [r5c9=r7c3=r9c7], [r6c8=r8c9], [r7c8=r8c2=r9c6]}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r9c7]‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {[r4c9=r9c7], r9c2, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r9c7], r8c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r4c9=r9c7], r7c8, r8c9}: [r4c9=r9c7], r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
              r7c3=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c9, r4c9, r9c7, r7c8} (source r8c9).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c2), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c5)
            row 5: r5c3, r5c9 (also r5c6)
            row 9: r9c2, [r4c9=r9c7] (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c8
            row 8: r8c2, [r6c8=r8c9]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c9‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c9‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c2], [r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]} (spine [r4c9=r9c7]‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color([r5c3=r9c2]) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c8=r8c9=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r7c3, r7c8} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c2), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color([r7c3=r9c6]). Identify.

              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r9c7], r8c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r4c9=r9c7], r7c8, r8c9}: [r4c9=r9c7], r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
              r7c3=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c9, r4c9, r9c7, r7c8} (source r8c9).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c1, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c1), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c1‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r7c3:

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, [r5c9=r7c3], [r8c9=r9c1], r9c6}:

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3], [r8c9=r9c1]}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c8}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {[r5c9=r7c3], r8c2, [r8c9=r9c1]}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX

              [r5c9=r7c3]=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c5)
            row 5: r5c3, r5c9 (also r5c6)
            row 9: r9c1, [r4c9=r9c7] (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c8
            row 8: r8c2, [r6c8=r8c9]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c9‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c1).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c9‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c1], [r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]} (spine [r4c9=r9c7]‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color([r5c3=r9c1]) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c8=r8c9=r9c1] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r7c3, r7c8} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c1, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c1), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c1‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===

            row 8: r8c2, r8c9
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c8, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c9‚Üîr9c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c9‚Üîr9c8 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r5c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), [r4c9=r9c8] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c3=r9c2], r5c9, r8c9, r7c7, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c8). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c8}
            Bridges: r8c9‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c2), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr9c8), r6c3‚Äîr6c8 (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c8‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).

        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c5)

      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.

  3.  Branch on r7c3, r9c6:
      Case A: color(r7c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, r5c6, [r7c3=r9c6]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color([r7c3=r9c6]). Identify.

            ‚Üí color(r8c2) = color(r9c6). Identify.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c5)
            row 5: r5c3, r5c9 (also r5c6)
            row 9: r9c1, [r4c9=r9c8] (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, r8c9
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c8, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c9‚Üîr9c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c1).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.

              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r5c3=r9c1]‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), [r4c9=r9c8] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.

            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c9, r8c9, r7c7, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c8). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c1, r9c8}
            Bridges: r8c9‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c1), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c1‚Äîr9c8), r6c3‚Äîr6c8 (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c8‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r8c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c9=X and r7c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r8c9, [r5c9=r7c3], r8c2, r9c1} (spine [r5c9=r7c3]‚Äîr8c2).
            ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, [r5c9=r7c3], [r8c9=r9c1], r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, [r8c9=r9c1]} and {[r5c9=r7c3], r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color([r8c9=r9c1]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r7c3]}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3]‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r8c9=r9c1], r9c6, r9c7}: [r8c9=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c3}: r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3], [r8c9=r9c1]}: [r8c9=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c8}: r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 7 {[r5c9=r7c3], r8c2, [r8c9=r9c1]}: [r8c9=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, [r8c9=r9c1], r9c7}: [r8c9=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c3=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c9=r7c3]) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r7c3]}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r8c9=r9c1], r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r8c9=r9c1], r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c3}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3], [r8c9=r9c1]}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c8}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {[r5c9=r7c3], r8c2, [r8c9=r9c1]}: [r5c9=r7c3]=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, [r8c9=r9c1], r9c7}: [r8c9=r9c1], r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
              [r5c9=r7c3]=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c1, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c7=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c9=X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9=X and r9c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX

              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r8c9‚âÝX

              r7c3=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c9=X and r7c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, [r6c8=r8c9] (also r6c5)
            row 5: r5c3, r5c9 (also r5c6)
            row 9: r9c1, [r4c9=r9c7] (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, [r6c8=r8c9]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c9‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c1).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c9‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c1], [r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c9]} (spine [r4c9=r9c7]‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color([r5c3=r9c1]) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c8=r8c9=r9c1] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r9c7], r5c9, r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r7c3, r7c7} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r5c9, r5c3, r7c3, r9c1, r9c7}
            Bridges: [r6c8=r8c9]‚Äîr8c2 (edges r4c9‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr9c1), r5c6‚Äîr9c6 (edges r5c9‚Äîr5c3 ‚Üî r9c1‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c9] (edges r5c3‚Äîr7c3 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, [r6c5=r8c6], r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr7c5 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äî[r6c5=r8c6]), r4c9‚Äîr6c8 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî [r6c5=r8c6]‚Äîr6c3), r9c2‚Äîr7c3 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c4 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r7c5], r7c3, r8c2, r8c6}:

              r6c3=X and r6c8=X are adjacent

              Contradiction.

            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r9c2 guards oddagon {r9c2, [r6c8=r9c9], r5c9, r5c3, r7c3} (source [r5c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r9c2) = color([r5c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c3=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c4, r8c6, r8c2, r7c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r7c5 guards oddagon {r7c5, r6c5, r5c6, r5c3, r7c3} (source r6c3).
            ‚Üí color(r7c5) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        6.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c3=r7c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c4, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:

      Case A: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]). Identify.

            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.

            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.

            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r5c6=r6c3], r6c5 (also [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9])

            Rows 5 and 9: r5c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4), r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c6=r6c3] and r9c4).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.

                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.

        3.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r7c5, r8c6} (spine r5c6‚Äîr7c5).
            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, [r6c5=r8c6]}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, [r6c5=r8c6]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r8c6], r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, [r6c5=r8c6]‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 8 {[r6c5=r8c6], r7c5, r9c4}: [r6c5=r8c6], r7c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              r9c4=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r8c6]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r8c6], r6c8}: [r6c5=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r6c5=r8c6], r7c5, r9c4}: [r6c5=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r7c5, r9c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c4, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r9c1 guards oddagon {r9c1, [r6c8=r9c9], r5c9, r5c3, r7c3} (source [r5c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r9c1) = color([r5c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c3=r9c1] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c4, r8c6, r8c2, r7c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r7c5 guards oddagon {r7c5, r6c5, r5c6, r5c3, r7c3} (source r6c3).
            ‚Üí color(r7c5) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        6.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c3=r7c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c4, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on [r6c8=r9c9], r7c3:
      Case A: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], r7c4, r8c6, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r6c8=r9c9]) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r7c3} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c9=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r6c3], [r5c9=r7c3], [r6c8=r9c9]} (spine [r5c6=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c9=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r5c6=r6c3], r6c5 (also [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9])
            row 5: [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9], [r5c6=r6c3] (also [r5c9=r7c3])
            row 9: r9c2, r9c4 (also [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9])
            row 7: [r5c9=r7c3], r7c4
            row 8: r8c2, r8c6
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c4, r7c4, r8c6} in box 8 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4), r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c6=r6c3] and r9c4).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c6=r6c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r6c8=r9c9], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c4], [r5c9=r7c3], r9c2}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {[r5c9=r8c6], r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c4], r5c6, [r5c9=r8c6]}: r5c6=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c4], [r5c9=r8c6]‚âÝX

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r7c4], r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, [r6c3=r7c4]‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r6c3=r7c4], r8c6, r9c4}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c1, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X and r9c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c4). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, r7c4, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr8c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr7c4), r4c9‚Äîr7c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r7c4‚Äîr6c3), r9c1‚Äîr7c3 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c4 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on [r6c8=r9c9], r7c3:
      Case A: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], r7c4, r8c6, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r6c8=r9c9]) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c9, r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r7c3} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r7c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c9=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r6c3], [r5c9=r7c3], [r6c8=r9c9]} (spine [r5c6=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c9=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r5c6=r6c3], r6c5 (also [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9])
            row 5: [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9], [r5c6=r6c3] (also [r5c9=r7c3])
            row 9: r9c1, r9c4 (also [r5c3=r6c8=r9c9])
            row 7: [r5c9=r7c3], r7c4
            row 8: r8c2, r8c6
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c4, r7c4, r8c6} in box 8 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4), r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c6=r6c3] and r9c4).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c6=r6c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r6c8=r9c9], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c4], [r5c9=r7c3], r9c1}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r7c4:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r7c4). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c3=r7c4], r5c6, r8c6} (spine [r5c3=r7c4]‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, [r5c9=r8c6], r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {[r5c9=r8c6], r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c4], r5c6, [r5c9=r8c6]}: r5c6=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c4], [r5c9=r8c6]‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r7c4], r6c3, r7c3}: [r5c3=r7c4], r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c6], r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c3=r7c4], [r5c9=r8c6], r9c4}: [r5c3=r7c4], [r5c9=r8c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              r9c4=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c6], r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c9=r8c6]) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c4], r5c6, [r5c9=r8c6]}: [r5c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c4], r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r7c4], r6c3, r7c3}: [r5c3=r7c4], r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r8c6], r9c9}: [r5c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c3=r7c4], [r5c9=r8c6], r9c4}: [r5c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c4, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c4). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c4, r8c6, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c6} and {r7c4, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c4, r8c6, r9c4}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r7c4, r9c4‚âÝX
              r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c1, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X and r9c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c4) = color(r8c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c4, r8c6, r9c4}: r7c4=X ‚Üí r8c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              r7c4=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c1, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r7c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c3=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, r5c9‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r6c3, r7c4:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r7c4). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r6c3=r7c4]‚Äîr7c3, r8c6‚Äîr8c2, r9c4‚Äîr9c1}:
            Satellites r5c3 (rung 1), r9c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c3=r7c4] guards oddagon {[r6c3=r7c4], r5c3, r9c9, r4c9, r6c8} (source r5c9).
            ‚Üí color([r6c3=r7c4]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r6c3=r7c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c6, r8c6, r9c4, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c3, r5c9, r9c9, r9c4, r7c4, r6c3}
            Bridges: r5c6‚Äîr8c6 (edges r5c3‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr7c4), r4c9‚Äîr7c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r7c4‚Äîr6c3), r9c1‚Äîr7c3 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c4 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr5c3).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.

      ‚Üí color(r8c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c7=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r7c9, r9c7, r9c2, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c7, r8c3, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c9, r4c9, [r8c7=r9c6], r8c3} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r8c7=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r7c9=r8c3], [r8c7=r9c6], r9c2, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r7c9 guards oddagon {r7c9, r4c9, r9c6, r8c5, r8c7} (source r8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r8c5], r8c3, r9c2, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r8c5], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r8c5]) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r8c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 8 {[r5c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c7}: [r5c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r8c5], r6c3, r8c3}: [r5c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r6c6=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {[r5c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r8c5], r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r8c5], r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r5c3=r8c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r8c5], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c5=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c3) = X

      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c6, r7c3, r8c2, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c3, r4c9, [r8c6=r9c2], r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r8c6=r9c2]).
      ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r7c3=r9c9], r7c5, [r8c6=r9c2], r9c4}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r9c4 guards oddagon {r9c4, r7c5, r8c6, r8c2, r9c2} (source r7c3).
      ‚Üí color(r9c4) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c3=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r8c6, r8c2, r9c2, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c2, r8c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r7c5], r8c2} and {r7c3, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r7c5]) = color(r8c2) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r7c5], r6c3, r7c3}: [r5c3=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r7c3‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c3=r7c5], r8c6, r9c4}: [r5c3=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r8c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r5c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              r6c6=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r7c5], r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c5], r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c3=r7c5], r8c6, r9c4}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r7c5], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r8c6, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c6} and {r7c5, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r7c5, r9c4‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c5) = color(r8c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r7c5=X ‚Üí r8c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              r7c5=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r7c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c2) = X

        r4c9=X and r8c6=X are adjacent

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c8)
      row 6: r6c3, r6c6 (also r6c7)
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5 (also r8c8)
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6), r5c8‚Üîr6c7 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c6 and r8c5).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r8c5], r8c3, r9c2, r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r8c5], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r5c8}: [r5c6=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r5c6=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: [r5c6=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        col 6 {[r5c6=r8c5], r6c6, r9c6}: [r5c6=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
        r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 8 {[r5c6=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c3=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r8c5], r8c8‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c7‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r5c6=r8c5], r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
        r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r5c8}: r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X and r6c7=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c8)
      row 6: r6c3, r6c6 (also r6c7)
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5 (also r8c8)
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6), r5c8‚Üîr6c7 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c6 and r8c5).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c6=r8c5] and [r5c6=r8c5]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c7}:
      Satellites r8c3 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c8, r8c3, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c8=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r7c9, r9c7, r9c2, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r7c5:

        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c2, r8c6}:

              Contradiction.

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r8c6, r8c2}:

        Contradiction.

      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r9c4 guards oddagon {r9c4, r7c5, r8c6, r8c2, r9c2} (source r7c3).
      ‚Üí color(r9c4) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c3=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r8c6, r8c2, r9c2, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c2, r8c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r7c5], r8c2} and {r7c3, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r7c5]) = color(r8c2) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r7c5], r6c3, r7c3}: [r5c3=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r7c3‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c3=r7c5], r8c6, r9c4}: [r5c3=r7c5]=X ‚Üí r8c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r5c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              r6c6=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r7c5], r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c5], r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r5c3=r7c5], r8c6, r9c4}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r7c5], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X and r6c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r8c6, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c6} and {r7c5, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r7c5, r9c4‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X and r6c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c5) = color(r8c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r7c5=X ‚Üí r8c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              r7c5=X ‚Üí r5c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r5c3, r7c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c6, r5c8‚Äîr6c7}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r7c5, r7c3, r8c6, r8c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color(r7c5) = color(r8c2). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r7c3, r9c2, r9c9}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r9c2} and {r7c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX
        box 7 {r7c3, [r7c5=r8c2], r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, [r7c5=r8c2]‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r7c5=r8c2], r8c6, r9c4}: [r7c5=r8c2], r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c6=X
        r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r4c9=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c4‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r7c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
        box 7 {r7c3, [r7c5=r8c2], r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí [r7c5=r8c2]‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r7c5=r8c2], r8c6, r9c4}: [r7c5=r8c2], r9c4‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c6=X
        r7c3=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r8c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c3=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        [r5c6=r8c4]=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
