      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c8‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c3=r8c8], [r7c7=r9c3], r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r8c8], [r7c7=r9c3]‚âÝX
        row 5 {[r5c3=r8c8], [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8}: [r5c3=r8c8], r5c8‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c6=r8c4]=X
        row 8 {[r5c3=r8c8], [r5c6=r8c4], r8c6}: [r5c6=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c3=r8c8], r6c3, [r7c7=r9c3]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r8c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8, r8c8} (spine [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r8c8], [r5c6=r8c4], r8c6} (spine [r5c3=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c3=r8c8], r9c3, r9c9} (spine [r5c3=r8c8]‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r8c6], r4c9, r6c7, [r6c3=r9c9]} (spine r4c9‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r8c6]) = color([r6c3=r9c9]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c4], [r5c3=r8c8], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c6=r9c9], r7c7} (spine [r5c3=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c3=r8c6=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  7.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r7c7=r8c4], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c6=r9c9], r6c5, r6c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r5c6, r7c6, r8c4, r8c8} (source r8c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c6‚Äîr6c5, [r5c8=r8c6]‚Äîr6c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r4c9‚Äîr9c3, [r5c8=r8c6]‚Äîr5c3, r6c7‚Äîr6c3}:
      Satellites r9c9 (rung 1), r5c6 (rung 2), r6c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r9c9, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r9c9} and {r5c6, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c8=r8c6]}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, [r5c8=r8c6]‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r9c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r5c6, [r5c8=r8c6], r7c6}: r5c6, [r5c8=r8c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
        box 8 {[r5c8=r8c6], r7c6, r8c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c7, r8c8‚âÝX
        r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
        row 8 {[r5c8=r8c6], r8c4, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c8=r8c6]}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, [r5c8=r8c6]‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r5c6, [r5c8=r8c6], r7c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r5c8=r8c6], r7c6, r8c4}: [r5c8=r8c6], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
        r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r8c4=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
        r6c7=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r8c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8, r8c8} (spine [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r6c7 guards oddagon {r6c7, r6c3, r9c3, r9c9, r7c7} (source [r5c3=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r5c3=r8c8]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c7=r8c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, [r5c6=r8c4], r6c5, r6c3, r9c3, r9c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5‚Äîr9c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c7], r9c5:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5], [r6c5=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5]‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        5.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 5: r5c6, r5c8 (also r5c3)
            row 6: [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r7c9] (also r6c2)
            row 9: [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r7c9]
            row 8: r8c5, [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c8 (col 8), r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5), r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c2 and r9c3).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c2) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r8c7=r9c5], [r6c2=r9c3], [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c8=r7c9]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c7]) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r8c5, [r4c9=r8c7], r9c5, r9c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr9c5).
            ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r7c9, [r4c9=r8c7], [r8c5=r9c9], r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äî[r8c5=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c7], [r7c9=r9c5], [r8c5=r9c9], [r6c5=r7c6]} (spine [r7c9=r9c5]‚Äî[r8c5=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c6, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7], r5c8, r6c8} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7]‚Äîr5c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7], [r5c6=r6c8], r6c2} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c8]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], [r6c5=r7c6], r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, [r6c5=r7c6]} and {[r5c6=r8c4], r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r7c6]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c6=r8c4], [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8}: [r5c6=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: r6c2=X ‚Üí [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c6=r8c4], [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6}: [r5c6=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c6), r6c2‚Äîr9c2 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr8c9 (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9, r7c7} (spine r8c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r7c7], r6c5:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r7c7]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c7], [r5c6=r8c4], r7c6} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c7]‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r7c6], [r4c9=r6c5=r7c7], [r5c6=r8c4], r8c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c7]‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r7c6]) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        6.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c9=r6c5=r7c7], r6c8 (also r6c2)
            row 5: [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9] (also r5c3)
            row 9: r9c6, r9c9 (also r9c2)
            row 7: [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9], [r4c9=r6c5=r7c7]
            row 8: [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c8 (col 8), r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c6, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6), r5c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        7.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r9c2], [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9], r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r7c7]) ‚âÝ color(r6c5). Add edge.
        8.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r7c7], r6c8, r6c5} (spine [r4c9=r7c7]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c4], r5c3, [r5c8=r6c5], r6c2} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r6c2=r8c4], [r5c8=r6c5], r6c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c2=r8c4]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r4c9=r7c7], [r5c3=r6c8], [r5c8=r6c5], [r5c6=r6c2=r8c4]} (spine [r5c3=r6c8]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r7c7]) = color([r5c6=r6c2=r8c4]). Identify.
        12.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2=r7c7=r8c4], r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c5 guards oddagon {r6c5, r5c6, r5c8, r6c8, r6c2} (source [r4c9=r5c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color([r4c9=r5c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c9, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r9c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              r9c2=X ‚Üí r6c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r6c2, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c4) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c8, r6c8}: [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X ‚Üí r6c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r4c9=X and r5c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r5c3], r6c8, r6c2} (spine [r4c9=r5c3]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c9, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX

              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c3], r5c6, [r5c8=r6c2]}: [r4c9=r5c3], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c2]=X
              row 6 {[r5c8=r6c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r5c8=r6c2]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX

              [r5c8=r6c2]=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c4) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c3], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c3]=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3], [r5c8=r6c2], r6c8}: [r4c9=r5c3]=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c2], r6c8‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c9=r5c3]=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X

              r4c9=X and r5c3=X are adjacent

            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c2, [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6], r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c5=r7c6] guards oddagon {[r6c5=r7c6], r7c7, r8c9, r4c9, r9c6} (source r9c9).
            ‚Üí color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c2, r9c2, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c6), r6c2‚Äîr9c2 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr8c9 (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r7c3], r8c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              r6c5=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c6=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r7c3]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c2], r5c6, [r5c8=r7c3]}: r5c6, [r5c8=r7c3]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c3=r9c2]=X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r7c3], r8c3}: [r5c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r7c3], r6c8}: [r5c8=r7c3], r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3, r7c3}
            Bridges: r4c9‚Äîr8c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr8c3), r6c2‚Äî[r5c3=r9c2] (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr7c3), r9c5‚Äî[r5c3=r9c2] (edges r6c5‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5‚Äîr9c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Branch on r4c9, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c3], [r5c3=r9c2], r7c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c3]‚Äî[r5c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c2], [r4c9=r8c3], [r5c8=r7c3], r6c8} (spine [r4c9=r8c3]‚Äî[r5c8=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c3=r9c2]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r6c8=r9c2], r5c6, [r5c8=r7c3], [r6c5=r7c6]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c3). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c3, r8c9, r8c5} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r8c5], [r6c5=r7c6], r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r8c5]‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c5], [r6c8=r9c5], [r6c5=r7c6]} (spine [r4c9=r8c5]‚Äî[r6c8=r9c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r6c5=r7c6], r6c2, [r6c8=r9c5]}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c2 guards oddagon {r6c2, r5c3, r5c8, r6c8, r6c5} (source [r4c9=r5c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c2) = color([r4c9=r5c6]). Identify.

            Diagonals: {r7c3, r9c5} and {r7c6, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r9c5) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r8c3‚âÝX

              r8c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c3, r5c8}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r6c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.

              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)

              r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c6). Add edge.

            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c3=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c2], r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c3). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c3, r8c9, r8c5} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c9).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r8c5], [r6c5=r7c6], r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r8c5]‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c5], [r6c8=r9c5], [r6c5=r7c6]} (spine [r4c9=r8c5]‚Äî[r6c8=r9c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r9c1], [r5c8=r6c5=r7c6], r6c2, [r6c8=r9c5]}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c2 guards oddagon {r6c2, r5c3, r5c8, r6c8, r6c5} (source [r4c9=r5c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c2) = color([r4c9=r5c6]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c6, r9c5, r9c1}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r9c5} and {r7c6, r9c1} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r9c5) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r9c5=X ‚Üí r6c5, r8c5‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r9c5=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c9=X
              r8c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c3, r5c8}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r6c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r9c1) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r9c1=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c9=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r8c5 guards oddagon {r8c5, r7c6, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3} (source [r5c8=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color([r5c8=r7c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c3=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3, r7c3}
            Bridges: r4c9‚Äîr8c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr8c3), r6c2‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr7c3), r9c5‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c5‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r9c2], r5c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r9c2]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c2, r6c5 (also r6c8)
      row 5: [r5c3=r9c2], r5c6 (also [r5c8=r9c6])
      row 9: [r5c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r9c6] (also r9c9)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c5
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c8 (col 8) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c3} in box 7, {r9c6, r7c5, r8c5} in box 8 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6), r5c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c3=r9c2] and [r5c3=r9c2]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c3=r9c2]) = color([r5c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, [r5c8=r9c6], r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r5c8=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r9c6], r7c5, r8c5, r6c5} (spine r7c5‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r9c6]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c5=r9c6], [r6c8=r9c9], r6c2} (spine [r5c8=r6c5=r9c6]‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c2). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5, r9c4} (spine r7c5‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r9c2], r5c6, [r6c5=r9c4]} (spine [r5c3=r9c2]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r9c4]). Identify.
  4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c2, [r5c8=r6c5=r9c4] (also r6c8)
      row 5: [r5c3=r9c2], r5c6 (also [r5c8=r6c5=r9c4])
      row 9: [r5c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r6c5=r9c4] (also r9c9)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c5
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c8 (col 8) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c3} in box 7, {r9c4, r7c5, r8c5} in box 8 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c3=r9c2] and [r5c3=r9c2]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.

          r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c3=r9c2]) = color([r5c3=r9c2]). Identify.

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c3=r9c1], r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c3=r9c1]) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 5 {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r9c1:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r9c1], r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r9c1]) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r5c3=r9c1], r9c4, r9c9}: [r5c3=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c1], r7c3, r8c3}: [r5c3=r9c1]=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r7c5, r8c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r9c1], r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {[r5c3=r9c1], r9c4, r9c9}: [r5c3=r9c1], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c4}: r9c4=X ‚Üí r7c5, r8c5‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c1). Add edge.
        3.  K‚ÇÑ on {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c2, [r5c3=r9c1], r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===

        2.  Guardian: r6c2 guards oddagon {r6c2, r5c3, r5c8, r6c8, r6c5} (source [r4c9=r5c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c2) = color([r4c9=r5c6]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c4, r9c5, r9c1}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c5} and {r8c4, r9c1} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c5) = X
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r7c3‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r9c5}: r9c5=X ‚Üí r6c5, r7c5‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c1‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c5}: r9c5=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              row 7 {r7c3, r7c5, r7c9}: r7c3, r7c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              r7c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c3, r5c8}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r6c5, r6c8}: [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r6c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c4) = color(r9c1) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r9c1=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c5}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              row 7 {r7c3, r7c5, r7c9}: r7c3, r7c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c9=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r9c5}: r7c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
              r5c3=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r9c5}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r7c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c1=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 7 {r7c3, r7c5, r7c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r4c9=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], [r5c8=r8c3], r7c3} (spine [r5c3=r9c1]‚Äî[r5c8=r8c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c3] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c3], [r5c6=r7c3], r6c5, r9c5, r8c4} (source r7c5).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r8c3]) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c5=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c5, [r5c6=r7c3], r7c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c3). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c5, r7c5, r7c3, r8c3}
            Bridges: r4c9‚Äîr7c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r7c5‚Äîr7c3), r6c2‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr8c3), r9c5‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c8)

      row 9: r9c2, r9c5 (also r9c8)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c7 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c8 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3), r5c6‚Üîr8c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c8 and r8c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr9: r5c8‚Üîr9c8 (col 8) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c8) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, r4c9, [r5c8=r8c7], r7c9} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r5c8=r8c7]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  3.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c8=r8c7]}
      row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, [r6c7=r7c9]}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, [r5c8=r8c7]}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c8}
      Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (box, box, box); row 6‚Üîrow 9 (col, col, box); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
      ‚Üí same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        7.  Diamond {r8c5, [r4c9=r8c8], r9c5, r9c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr9c5).
            ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r7c9, [r4c9=r8c8], [r8c5=r9c9], r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äî[r8c5=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c8], [r7c9=r9c5], [r8c5=r9c9], [r6c5=r7c6]} (spine [r7c9=r9c5]‚Äî[r8c5=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c6, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8], r5c8, r6c7} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8]‚Äîr5c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8], [r5c6=r6c7], r6c2} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c7]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8], [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c2], r5c3} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c2]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8]) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        13.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5=r7c6=r8c8], [r7c9=r9c5], [r8c5=r9c9], r9c3}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_hexagon] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r4c9 guards oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c3, r9c3, r9c9} (source [r5c6=r9c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color([r5c6=r9c5]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r5c6=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r8c5, r7c6, r7c9, r9c9, r9c3} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c7‚âÝX
              col 5 {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r6c5, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c8=X
              r8c5=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r8c5, r9c5, r7c6} (spine r8c5‚Äîr9c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c7], r9c5:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r6c7, [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5], [r6c5=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5]‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        5.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 5: r5c6, r5c8 (also r5c3)
            row 6: [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c7=r7c9] (also r6c2)
            row 9: [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c7=r7c9]
            row 8: r8c5, [r4c9=r8c7=r9c5]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c7 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5), r6c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c2 and r9c3).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c2) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r8c7=r9c5], [r6c2=r9c3], [r6c5=r7c6], [r6c7=r7c9]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c7]) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r8c5, [r4c9=r8c7], r9c5, r9c9} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr9c5).
            ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r7c9, [r4c9=r8c7], [r8c5=r9c9], r9c5} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äî[r8c5=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c7], [r7c9=r9c5], [r8c5=r9c9], [r6c5=r7c6]} (spine [r7c9=r9c5]‚Äî[r8c5=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color([r6c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c6, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7], r5c8, r6c7} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7]‚Äîr5c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7], [r5c6=r6c7], r6c2} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c7]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], [r6c5=r7c6], r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, [r6c5=r7c6]} and {[r5c6=r8c4], r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color([r6c5=r7c6]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c7}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c6=r8c4], [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c4], r5c8}: [r5c6=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c7}: r6c2=X ‚Üí [r6c5=r7c6], r6c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c6=r8c4], [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6}: [r5c6=r8c4]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c6), r6c2‚Äîr9c2 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr8c9 (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9, r7c8} (spine r8c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r7c8], r6c5:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r7c8]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r6c5=r7c8], [r5c6=r8c4], r7c6} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c8]‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r7c6], [r4c9=r6c5=r7c8], [r5c6=r8c4], r8c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c5=r7c8]‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r7c6]) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        6.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c9=r6c5=r7c8], r6c7 (also r6c2)
            row 5: [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9] (also r5c3)
            row 9: r9c6, r9c9 (also r9c2)
            row 7: [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9], [r4c9=r6c5=r7c8]
            row 8: [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9]
            Rows 5 and 6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c7 (box 6), r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c6, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6), r5c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 5, row 6, row 7, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí same Œ¥, pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        7.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r9c2], [r5c6=r8c4], [r5c8=r7c6=r8c9], r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r7c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c5). Add edge.
        8.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r7c8], r6c7, r6c5} (spine [r4c9=r7c8]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c4], r5c3, [r5c8=r6c5], r6c2} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r6c2=r8c4], [r5c8=r6c5], r6c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c2=r8c4]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r4c9=r7c8], [r5c3=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c5], [r5c6=r6c2=r8c4]} (spine [r5c3=r6c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r7c8]) = color([r5c6=r6c2=r8c4]). Identify.
        12.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2=r7c8=r8c4], r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c5 guards oddagon {r6c5, r5c6, r5c8, r6c7, r6c2} (source [r4c9=r5c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color([r4c9=r5c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c8, r8c9, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c8, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r9c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX
              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c6, r5c8}: [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX
              r9c2=X ‚Üí r6c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r6c2, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c4) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c9}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              r7c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5], r5c8, r6c7}: [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX
              [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X ‚Üí r6c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r4c9=X and r5c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c4=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)

              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r5c3], r6c7, r6c2} (spine [r4c9=r5c3]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c8, r8c9, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c8, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX

              [r5c8=r6c2]=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              r7c8=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)

              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3], [r5c8=r6c2], r6c7}: [r4c9=r5c3]=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c9=r5c3]=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {r5c3‚Äîr6c2, [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äî[r6c5=r7c6], r5c8‚Äîr6c7}:
            Satellites r9c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r6c5=r7c6] guards oddagon {[r6c5=r7c6], r7c8, r8c9, r4c9, r9c6} (source r9c9).
            ‚Üí color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c2, r9c2, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c6), r6c2‚Äîr9c2 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr8c9 (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              r7c8=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c6]‚âÝX (adjacent)

              col 6 {[r5c3=r7c6], r5c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c2=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 2 {r6c2, r8c2, r9c2}: r6c2=X and r9c2=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r7c6], r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r7c6], r5c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c3=r7c6], r5c6, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c6, r7c6, r9c6} (spine r5c6‚Äîr7c6).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r5c3=r9c6], r6c2, r9c2, r8c2} (spine r6c2‚Äîr9c2).
            ‚Üí color([r5c3=r9c6]) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c7, r8c7, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c7} and {r6c7, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c7) = X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX
              r8c7=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r8c2=r9c6]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r5c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              row 9 {[r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r9c2, r9c9}: [r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 2 {[r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r6c2, r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r6c2‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r5c6, r7c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c5‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r9c2‚âÝX
              col 2 {[r5c3=r8c2=r9c6], r6c2, r9c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r6c5], r5c8, r6c7} (spine [r5c3=r6c5]‚Äîr5c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r6c5], [r5c6=r6c7], r6c2} (spine [r5c3=r6c5]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c7]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c6=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c2], [r5c3=r6c5]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c2]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color([r5c3=r6c5]). Identify.
        5.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r5c3=r6c5]‚Äîr9c9, [r5c6=r6c7]‚Äîr9c6, [r5c8=r6c2]‚Äîr9c2}:
            Satellites r7c6 (rung 2), r8c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Trivalue oddagon:
            box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}
              ‚Üí via rows {8, 9} [even]
            col 2 {[r5c8=r6c2], r8c2, r9c2}
              ‚Üí via rows {9} [even]
            col 6 {[r5c6=r6c7], r7c6, r9c6}
              ‚Üí via rows {7, 9} [odd]
            Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r6c5). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c3, r5c6, r6c5} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c8=r6c5], r6c2} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r6c2], [r5c8=r6c5], r6c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c2]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c3=r6c7], [r5c8=r6c5], [r5c6=r6c2]} (spine [r5c3=r6c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color([r5c6=r6c2]). Identify.
        11.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c6=r6c2], r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r4c9=X and r5c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX

              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c3:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r5c3], r6c7, r6c2} (spine [r4c9=r5c3]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c9, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c7, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX

              row 5 {[r4c9=r5c3], r5c6, [r5c8=r6c2]}: [r4c9=r5c3], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c2]=X
              row 6 {[r5c8=r6c2], r6c5, r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c2]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX

              [r5c8=r6c2]=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c4) = X

              r7c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c3], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r5c3]=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r5c3], [r5c8=r6c2], r6c7}: [r4c9=r5c3]=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c2]‚âÝX
              r5c6=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c9=r5c3]=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).

              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c2, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c7, r7c7} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r6c2) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c6=r8c4] guards oddagon {[r5c6=r8c4], r8c9, [r6c2=r7c7], r9c2, r9c6} (source r9c9).
            ‚Üí color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r8c4=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c3, [r6c2=r7c7], r8c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) ‚âÝ color(r7c6). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r7c6, r9c6, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c6=r8c4] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c6), r6c2‚Äîr9c2 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c9), [r5c6=r8c4]‚Äîr8c9 (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              [r4c9=r5c6]=X and r8c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              r9c2=X ‚Üí r6c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c9=X

              Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r8c5 guards oddagon {r8c5, r7c6, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c2], r8c3} (source [r5c8=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color([r5c8=r7c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c3=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c2], r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3, r7c3}
            Bridges: r4c9‚Äîr8c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr8c3), r6c2‚Äî[r5c3=r9c2] (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr7c3), r9c5‚Äî[r5c3=r9c2] (edges r6c5‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r8c5 guards oddagon {r8c5, r7c6, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3} (source [r5c8=r7c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color([r5c8=r7c3]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c3=r8c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c3). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3, r7c3}
            Bridges: r4c9‚Äîr8c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr8c3), r6c2‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr7c3), r9c5‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c5‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r6c2}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c5} and {r5c6, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c7‚âÝX
        col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
        col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r7c5, r8c5‚âÝX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c5, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
        r4c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c2=X and r9c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
        r4c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r5c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r6c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r9c2], r5c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r9c2]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c2, r6c5 (also r6c7)
      row 5: [r5c3=r9c2], r5c6 (also [r5c8=r9c6])
      row 9: [r5c3=r9c2], [r5c8=r9c6] (also r9c9)
      row 7: r7c3, r7c5
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c8‚Üîr6c7 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c3} in box 7, {r9c6, r7c5, r8c5} in box 8 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6), r5c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r5c3=r9c2] and [r5c3=r9c2]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c5‚Üîr7c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r5c3=r9c2]) = color([r5c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c7, r4c9, [r5c8=r9c6], r9c9} (spine r4c9‚Äî[r5c8=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r9c9). Identify.

      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r9c6]) = color(r6c5). Identify.

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c4}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6=X ‚Üí [r5c3=r9c1], r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c7‚âÝX

              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c1). Add edge.
        3.  K‚ÇÑ on {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5, r9c4} (spine r7c5‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c3=r9c1], r5c6, [r6c5=r9c4]} (spine [r5c3=r9c1]‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r6c5=r9c4]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c5=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c2, [r5c3=r9c1], r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c1} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r5c3=r9c1], [r5c8=r8c3], r7c3} (spine [r5c3=r9c1]‚Äî[r5c8=r8c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c3] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c3], [r5c6=r7c3], r6c5, r9c5, r8c4} (source r7c5).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r8c3]) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r7c5=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c7, r6c5, [r5c6=r7c3], r7c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c3). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r7c5, r7c3, r8c3}
            Bridges: r4c9‚Äîr7c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r7c5‚Äîr7c3), r6c2‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r7c3‚Äîr8c3), r9c5‚Äî[r5c3=r9c1] (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c5 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r4c6, r5c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 4: r4c6, r4c9
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9
      row 6: [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8 (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c4, r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: [r6c5=r7c6], r7c8
      row 8: r8c5, r8c9
      Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 7 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 4, row 5, row 6, row 7 pairwise distinct:
          r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r4‚Üîr7: r4c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°
