Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c8, r8c9, r8c4}:
      Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c9} and {r7c8, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c9) = X
        col 6 {r4c6, r5c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r4c6, r5c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX
        box 5 {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5}: r4c6, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r9c5‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c7‚âÝX
        r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r6c5=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c4) = X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c7‚âÝX
        r7c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
        r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c5=X
        box 5 {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r5c6‚âÝX
        col 6 {r4c6, r5c6, r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 4: r4c6, r4c9
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9
      row 6: r6c5, r6c8 (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c5, r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c8
      row 8: r8c4, r8c9
      Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c8, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 7 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 4, row 5, row 6, row 7 pairwise distinct:
          r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r4‚Üîr7: r4c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c8‚Üîr7c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r4c6, r5c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr5c6).

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: [r6c5=r7c6]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c4) = X
        box 8 {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c4, r9c5}: r8c4=X ‚Üí [r6c5=r7c6], r9c5‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c8‚âÝX

        r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X

        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 4: r4c6, r4c9
      row 5: r5c6, r5c9
      row 6: r6c5, r6c8 (also r6c3)
      row 9: r9c5, r9c7 (also r9c3)
      row 7: r7c6, r7c7
      row 8: r8c4, r8c9
      Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c9} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 7, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 4, row 5, row 7, row 8 pairwise distinct:
          r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r4‚Üîr7: r4c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r4‚Üîr8: r4c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr7: r5c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        3.  K‚ÇÑ on {r4c6, r5c6, r6c5, r7c6}. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+parity+branch) ===

                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c6=r9c5], r8c8} and {[r6c8=r9c9], r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c6=r9c5]) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r5c5, r8c5‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r7c9, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r9c5]=X and r4c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r7c9}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9, r7c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c9, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c9), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r4c6=r6c3], r6c8, r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r6c3]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c9, r8c8, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c8) = X

              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c6], r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c5) = X
              col 5 {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r5c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c6], r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c6], r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3]=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c8, r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:

            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c8 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r9c3], r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c5], r6c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX

              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              col 5 {r5c5, r8c5, r9c5}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX

              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_parity] (SET+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  SET: {col 6, col 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r8c8} = {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r8c5}.
            ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c8, r8c8, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c8} and {r6c8, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c7‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], r9c3, r9c7}: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5}: [r4c6=r9c5], r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r5c5‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r8c5} (spine [r4c6=r9c5]‚Äîr5c5).
            ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c5], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c5], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c5]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c6, r9c4:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c6=r9c4], [r5c5=r7c6], r8c5} (spine [r4c6=r9c4]‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c4], r4c9
            row 5: [r5c5=r7c6], r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c5], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c4], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c5], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c5]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c4}
            Bridges: [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c4), r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c7], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c7], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r7c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c7]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c6=r8c7], r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c7]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c7], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r7c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], r9c9} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9], r9c3} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9]).

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_parity] (SET+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c6, r8c5:

        2.  SET: {col 5, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 5, box 9}.

            ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r9c9, r8c7}:

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r8c7) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX

              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c6=r8c5], r6c8}: [r6c6=r8c5], r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 5 {r5c5, [r6c6=r8c5], r9c5}: [r6c6=r8c5], r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              col 6 {r4c6, [r6c6=r8c5], r7c6}: r4c6, [r6c6=r8c5]‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c5, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr9c5), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr9c9), r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r4c6, r5c5, [r6c6=r8c5], r9c5} (spine r5c5‚Äî[r6c6=r8c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c5], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c5], r8c7
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c9).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr9: r4c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r7c9, r8c7, [r6c6=r8c5]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c5, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr9c5), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr9c9), r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

                r4‚Üîr9: r4c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.

                r5‚Üîr9: r5c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r7c9, r8c7, [r6c6=r8c5]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c5, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            row 6: r6c6, r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c9
            row 8: r8c4, r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c9).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c6=r9c5], r8c8} and {[r6c8=r9c9], r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c6=r9c5]) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r7c9, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r9c5]=X and r4c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, [r6c8=r9c9], r7c9}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9, r7c9‚âÝX

            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            row 6: r6c6, r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c9
            row 8: r8c4, r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c7], r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c6=r9c5], r8c8} and {[r6c8=r9c7], r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c6=r9c5]) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c7], r9c3}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r9c7], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c7]}: r5c8, [r6c8=r9c7]‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r9c5]=X and r4c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r9c7]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r9c7]}: [r6c8=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c7], r9c3}: [r6c8=r9c7]=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c7], r8c8}: [r6c8=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c7]}: [r6c8=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: r7c6=X and r8c4=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c8], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r7c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c6=r8c8], r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r7c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c8], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c8], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c6=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c8], r9c7} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r6c6=r8c8]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c8], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c7], r9c3} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c6=r8c8]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        13.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c8=r9c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c7], r8c4, r9c5}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r4c6=r6c3], r6c8, r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r6c3]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c9, r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6]‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c6], r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c4) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c6]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6}: [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3]=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r9c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_parity] (SET+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  SET: {col 6, col 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r8c8} = {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r8c4}.
            ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c8, r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c8} and {r6c8, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c7‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], r9c3, r9c7}: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: r8c4=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r7c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4} (spine [r4c6=r9c5]‚Äîr7c6).
            ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c4], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c4]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              Contradiction.

              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6}: [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3]=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c8], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r7c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c6=r8c8], r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r7c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c8], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c8], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c6=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c8], r9c7} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r6c6=r8c8]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c8], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c7], r9c3} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c6=r8c8]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        13.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c8=r9c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c7], r8c4, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r5c5} (source [r4c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r4c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c9, r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c7‚âÝX
              row 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c4) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c4, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r9c3=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              col 8 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c8, r8c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: r6c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              r6c6=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX

              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c8=X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c6, r9c4:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c6=r9c4], [r5c5=r7c6], r8c4} (spine [r4c6=r9c4]‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c4], r4c9
            row 5: [r5c5=r7c6], r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c4], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c4], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c4]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c4}
            Bridges: [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c4), r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c7], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c7], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r7c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c7]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c6=r8c7], r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c7]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c7], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r7c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], r9c9} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9], r9c3} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        13.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7=r9c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9], r8c4, r9c5}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r5c5} (source [r4c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r4c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c9, r8c7, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c7} and {r7c9, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c7) = X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: r4c9=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c4) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r9c3=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: r6c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              r6c6=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r9c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X

              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c4], r9c5} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9
            row 5: [r5c5=r7c6], r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c4], r8c7
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c9).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr9: r4c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r7c9, r8c7, [r6c6=r8c4]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c4, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr9c5), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c4 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        11.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], r9c9} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c8=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9], r9c3} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        13.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7=r9c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r7c6=r9c9], r8c4, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r5c5} (source [r4c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r4c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c9, r8c7, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c7} and {r7c9, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c7) = X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: r4c9=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c4) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r9c3=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: r6c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              r6c6=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c4:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c4], r9c4} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c4]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c4], r4c9
            row 5: [r5c5=r7c6], r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c4], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c4], r8c7
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c9).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr9: r4c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r7c9, r8c7, [r6c6=r8c4]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c4, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c4‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c4 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: [r4c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r8c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r7c7, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              [r4c6=r9c5]=X and r4c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]}: [r6c8=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: r7c6=X and r8c4=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c9, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c7 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c9), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r4c6=r6c3], r6c8, r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r6c3]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c7, r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c8} and {r7c7, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6]‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c6], r5c8, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c4) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r8c4=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c9‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r9c3=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6}: [r4c6=r6c3], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c6]=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c9=r6c6]=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c6], r5c8, r6c8}: [r4c9=r6c6]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX

              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:

            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c9=r9c3], r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c4], r6c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r9c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_parity] (SET+hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  SET: {col 6, col 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8}.
            Remainder: {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r8c8} = {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r8c4}.
            ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c8, r8c8, r8c4}:
            Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c8} and {r6c8, r8c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c7‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r8c4‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {[r4c6=r9c5], r9c3, r9c7}: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c5], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r9c5], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {[r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4}: r8c4=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c5], r7c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r4c6=r9c5], r6c6, r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c7 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c4} (spine [r4c6=r9c5]‚Äîr7c6).
            ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c5], r4c9
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c5], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, r7c7
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c4], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c5], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c4]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c5}
            Bridges: r7c6‚Äîr7c7 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c5), r5c8‚Äîr5c5 (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°
