            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c7, r9c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c6, r9c4:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c6=r9c4], [r5c5=r7c6], r8c4} (spine [r4c6=r9c4]‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c4], r4c9
            row 5: [r5c5=r7c6], r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c4], r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c4], r9c7 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], r7c7
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c4], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c4} in box 8, {r9c7, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c7).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c6‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c4]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c4). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c8, r8c8, r9c7, r9c4}
            Bridges: [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c7 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c8‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr9c4), r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r6c8‚Äîr8c8 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c8], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r9c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c8=r9c6], r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r9c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r9c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, [r4c9=r8c8]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3] and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r4c9=r8c8], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6], r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r7c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, [r6c8=r7c9]
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9], r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r7c9], r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c9}: [r6c8=r7c9], r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              r9c9=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r7c9], r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c9}: [r6c8=r7c9], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c8=X
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2]=X
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c9}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c9). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c9, r8c8, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r4c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c2) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c8, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r6c3, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r9c2} (source [r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c5=r9c6], r5c8, r8c8, r8c2, r9c2} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r4c6‚Äîr4c9, [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r9c2 (rung 2), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Gradient chain:
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4), r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            ‚Üí row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
              r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
            4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r7c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c7 (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, [r6c8=r7c9]
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9], r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r7c9], r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c7}: [r6c8=r7c9], r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c7=X
              r9c7=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9]‚âÝX
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2]=X
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c9). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c9, r8c8, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c7‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r4c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c7‚âÝX
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c8]‚Äî[r5c8=r8c2], r7c9‚Äîr7c3, r9c7‚Äîr9c2}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c2, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c2, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr9c7 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c2), r6c3‚Äîr7c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr8c2), r4c6‚Äî[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6‚Äîr9c6 ‚Üî r8c2‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c8], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r9c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c8=r9c6], r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r9c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r9c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c1, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, [r4c9=r8c8]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3] and r9c1).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c1], [r4c9=r8c8], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6], r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r7c9:

        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):

            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX

              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r7c9). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c9, r8c8, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r4c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c1, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c2) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c1‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c1, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c8, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r6c3, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r9c1} (source [r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c5=r9c6], r5c8, r8c8, r8c2, r9c1} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r4c6‚Äîr4c9, [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r9c1 (rung 2), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Gradient chain:
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c1, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4), r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            ‚Üí row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
              r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
            4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c8], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r9c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c8=r9c6], r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r9c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r9c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c1, r9c7 (also [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, [r4c9=r8c8]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c7, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3] and r9c1).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c1], [r4c9=r8c8], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6], r9c7}. Contradiction.

  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c7], [r5c8=r8c2], r7c3, r7c9}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r8c7], r7c3} and {[r5c8=r8c2], r7c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r7c3) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c7], r7c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c9=r8c7], [r5c8=r8c2], r6c8}: [r4c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r8c2], r6c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {[r5c8=r8c2], r7c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              [r4c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r4c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r7c9) = X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c7], r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí [r4c9=r8c7], r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c9=r8c7], [r5c8=r8c2], r6c8}: [r5c8=r8c2]=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {[r5c8=r8c2], r7c3, r9c2}: [r5c8=r8c2]=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX
              [r5c8=r8c2]=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c2). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c2, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr9c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c2), r6c3‚Äîr7c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c2‚Äîr8c2), r4c6‚Äî[r4c9=r8c7] (edges r6c6‚Äîr9c6 ‚Üî r8c2‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c7], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c7], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c7]) = color([r5c5=r9c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c7=r9c6], r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c7]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c7], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r9c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r9c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, [r4c9=r8c7]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3] and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r4c9=r8c7], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6], r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r9c9 guards oddagon {r9c9, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6} (source [r4c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r9c9) = color([r4c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c6=r6c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c2, r7c3, r7c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c1‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c2:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c2). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c9=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r8c2], r7c9‚Äîr7c3, r9c9‚Äîr9c1}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r5c8=r8c2] guards oddagon {[r5c8=r8c2], r5c5, r4c6, r9c6, r6c8} (source r6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c1, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c1, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr9c9 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c1), r6c3‚Äîr7c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c1‚Äîr8c2), r4c6‚Äî[r4c9=r8c7] (edges r6c6‚Äîr9c6 ‚Üî r8c2‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c6, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c6=r9c7], [r4c9=r7c8], r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c6=r9c7], r5c8} and {[r4c9=r7c8], r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c6=r9c7]) = color(r5c8) = X

              Contradiction.

              r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r7c8], [r6c3=r9c2]:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r7c8]) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r7c8=r9c2], r4c6, r6c6, r5c5} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r7c8=r9c2]) = color(r5c5). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c8=r9c2], r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r7c8]) ‚âÝ color([r6c3=r9c2]). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r7c8], [r6c3=r9c2], r7c3} (spine [r4c9=r7c8]‚Äî[r6c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c3], [r4c9=r7c8], [r6c3=r9c2], r9c7} (spine [r4c9=r7c8]‚Äî[r6c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c3]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        8.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 8: r8c5, r8c9 (also r8c3)
            row 6: r6c6, [r6c8=r7c3=r9c7] (also [r6c3=r9c2])
            row 4: r4c6, [r4c9=r7c8]
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            Rows 6, 4, 5: {r6c6, r4c6, r5c5} in box 5, {r6c8, r4c9, r5c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 8: r6c8‚Üîr8c9 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r8c5).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r8c5], [r6c8=r7c3=r9c7], r8c3}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r8c9:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 8: r8c5, [r5c8=r8c9] (also r8c3)
            row 6: r6c6, r6c8 (also r6c3)
            row 4: r4c6, r4c9
            row 5: r5c5, [r5c8=r8c9]
            Rows 6, 4, 5: {r6c6, r4c6, r5c5} in box 5, {r6c8, r4c9, r5c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 8: r6c8‚Üîr8c9 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r8c5).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c8=r8c9], r7c8, r7c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c8=r8c9], r7c3} and {r7c8, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c3) = X
              row 8 {[r5c8=r8c9], [r6c6=r8c5], r8c3}: [r5c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí [r6c6=r8c5], r8c3‚âÝX

              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c8}: [r5c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX

              col 8 {[r4c9=r7c8], r5c8, r6c8}: [r4c9=r7c8]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r9c9], r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r9c9], r6c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r4c6=r9c9], [r4c9=r7c8], r8c7}: [r4c9=r7c8]=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
              [r4c9=r7c8]=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X and r8c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r8c7, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c5‚Äîr8c5 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr8c7), r6c8‚Äî[r4c9=r7c8] (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c3 ‚Üî r8c7‚Äîr9c9), r7c3‚Äî[r4c9=r7c8] (edges r6c3‚Äîr8c3 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r7c8], [r6c3=r9c2]:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r7c8]) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r7c8=r9c2], r4c6, r6c6, r5c5} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r7c8=r9c2]) = color(r5c5). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c8=r9c2], r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r7c8]) ‚âÝ color([r6c3=r9c2]). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r7c8], [r6c3=r9c2], r7c3} (spine [r4c9=r7c8]‚Äî[r6c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c3], [r4c9=r7c8], [r6c3=r9c2], r9c9} (spine [r4c9=r7c8]‚Äî[r6c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        8.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 8: r8c5, r8c7 (also r8c3)
            row 6: r6c6, [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9] (also [r6c3=r9c2])
            row 4: r4c6, [r4c9=r7c8]
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            Rows 6, 4, 5: {r6c6, r4c6, r5c5} in box 5, {r6c8, r4c9, r5c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 8: r6c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r8c5).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c9‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c7 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r8c5], [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], r8c3}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).

        4.  Guardian: [r4c6=r9c9] guards oddagon {[r4c6=r9c9], r8c7, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2} (source r8c3).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c6=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c5, r6c6, r6c3, r8c7, r8c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r8c7, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c5‚Äîr8c5 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr8c7), r6c8‚Äî[r4c9=r7c8] (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c3 ‚Üî r8c7‚Äîr9c9), r7c3‚Äî[r4c9=r7c8] (edges r6c3‚Äîr8c3 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9, r7c7} (spine r8c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r7c7], [r6c3=r9c2]:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r7c7]) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r7c7=r9c2], r4c6, r6c6, r5c5} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r7c7=r9c2]) = color(r5c5). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c7=r9c2], r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r7c7]) ‚âÝ color([r6c3=r9c2]). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r6c8, [r4c9=r7c7], [r6c3=r9c2], r7c3} (spine [r4c9=r7c7]‚Äî[r6c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c3], [r4c9=r7c7], [r6c3=r9c2], r9c9} (spine [r4c9=r7c7]‚Äî[r6c3=r9c2]).
            ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        8.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 8: r8c5, r8c9 (also r8c3)
            row 6: r6c6, [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9] (also [r6c3=r9c2])
            row 4: r4c6, [r4c9=r7c7]
            row 5: r5c5, r5c8
            Rows 6, 4, 5: {r6c6, r4c6, r5c5} in box 5, {r6c8, r4c9, r5c8} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 8: r6c8‚Üîr8c9 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r8c5).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 8 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr8: r4c9‚Üîr8c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr8: r5c5‚Üîr8c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r8c5], [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], r8c3}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6, r5c5} (source [r4c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r4c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c7, r9c9, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r9c9} and {r7c7, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              col 3 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r8c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r8c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r5c5=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r9c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c5=X
              col 3 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]=X
              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r5c5, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r4c9, r6c8}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]=X and r4c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c8‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r5c5) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c7=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X and r8c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c7, r9c9, r9c2}:

              Contradiction.

              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX

              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c6], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c9=r6c6]=X
              box 5 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r5c5}: [r4c6=r6c3]=X and [r4c9=r6c6]=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9, r7c7} (spine r8c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c6, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Circular ladder {[r4c6=r9c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r7c7], r5c5‚Äîr5c8, r6c6‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r8c9 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: [r4c6=r9c9] guards oddagon {[r4c6=r9c9], r8c9, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2} (source r8c3).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c6=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c5, r6c6, r6c3, r8c9, r8c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c6, r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r8c9, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c5‚Äîr8c5 (edges r4c6‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr8c9), r6c8‚Äî[r4c9=r7c7] (edges r6c6‚Äîr6c3 ‚Üî r8c9‚Äîr9c9), r7c3‚Äî[r4c9=r7c7] (edges r6c3‚Äîr8c3 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c8], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color([r5c5=r9c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c8=r9c6], r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c8]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr6c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r9c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c8} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r9c6]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        9.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6] (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, [r4c9=r8c8]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3] and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r4c9=r8c8], [r5c5=r6c8=r9c6], r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c7, r8c8, r8c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c8} and {r7c7, r8c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r4c9=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X ‚Üí r8c2, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r7c7, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              r4c9=X ‚Üí r4c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r6c3, [r6c8=r9c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c6}: [r6c8=r9c9], r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r8c2) = X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r8c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r9c2‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r7c7, r8c8}: r7c7=X ‚Üí [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r6c8=r9c9], r9c2, r9c6}: [r6c8=r9c9], r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8}: [r6c8=r9c9], r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c3 and r9c2).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r6c8} and {r4c9, r6c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r6c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r6c6) = X

              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c8, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r6c3, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r9c2} (source [r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c5=r9c6], r5c8, r8c8, r8c2, r9c2} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        5.  Circular ladder {r4c6‚Äîr4c9, [r5c5=r9c6]‚Äîr9c9, r6c6‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r9c2 (rung 2), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Gradient chain:
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c2, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4), r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            ‚Üí row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
              r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c2 (col 2) ‚Üí pairs differ.
            4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, [r6c8=r9c9]}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9‚âÝX
              r5c5=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c1, r9c9 (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c9, r7c7, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c3 and r9c1).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c3) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r6c8} and {r4c9, r6c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r9c1], r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c1], r6c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r8c8‚âÝX

              r6c8=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {[r6c3=r9c1], r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r6c6) = X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+parity+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).

  2.  Branch on r6c8, r9c9:

        3.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r6c3, r6c6, [r5c5=r9c6], r9c1} (source [r6c8=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r7c3) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c5=r9c6], r5c8, r8c8, r8c2, r9c1} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.

            Satellites r9c1 (rung 2), r6c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Gradient chain:
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c8 (also r6c6)
            row 9: r9c1, r9c9 (also [r5c5=r9c6])
            row 7: r7c3, r7c7
            row 8: r8c2, r8c8

            ‚Üí row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
              r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.

              r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
            4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r8c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6=r8c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c8, r9c6, r9c1, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c2). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c1, r8c2}
            Bridges: [r4c9=r8c8]‚Äîr9c7 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c1), r6c3‚Äîr7c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c1‚Äîr8c2), r4c6‚Äî[r4c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6‚Äîr9c6 ‚Üî r8c2‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c8], r6c3:

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c5], r9c4} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 4: [r4c6=r9c4], r4c9
            row 5: [r5c5=r7c6], r5c8
            row 6: [r6c6=r8c5], r6c7 (also r6c3)
            row 9: [r4c6=r9c4], r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: [r5c5=r7c6], r7c9
            row 8: [r6c6=r8c5], r8c8
            Rows 4, 5, 6: {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6} in box 5, {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7} in box 6 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r9c9).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 4, row 5, row 6, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r4‚Üîr5: r4c6‚Üîr5c5 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr6: r4c6‚Üîr6c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r4‚Üîr9: r4c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr6: r5c5‚Üîr6c6 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r5‚Üîr9: r5c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r5c8, r8c8, [r6c6=r8c5]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c6, r8c5, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
              r8c5=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6}: [r4c9=r6c6], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí [r4c6=r6c3]=X
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              [r4c6=r6c3]=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c6) = color(r5c8) = X
              col 6 {r4c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {r4c6, r5c5, r6c6}: r4c6=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r4c9, r6c7‚âÝX
              r4c6=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Branch on [r4c9=r8c7], r6c3:
      Case A: color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c3=r8c7], r4c6, r6c6, [r5c5=r7c6]} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c3=r8c7]) = color([r5c5=r7c6]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r5c5=r6c3=r7c6=r8c7], r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r4c9=r8c7]) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r4c9=r8c7], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r4c9=r8c7]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r5c8=r6c3]} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3]). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {[r5c5=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c7} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr6c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r7c6]) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {[r4c9=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r5c5=r6c7=r7c6], r6c6} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c7=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c5=r6c7=r7c6], r7c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c7=r7c6]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c7=r7c6], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], r9c9} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]).
            ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c7=r7c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        12.  Diamond {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9], [r5c5=r6c7=r7c6=r9c9], r9c3} (spine [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3=r7c9]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c7=r7c6=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color([r4c9=r6c6=r8c7]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        13.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r4c9=r6c6=r8c7=r9c3], [r5c5=r6c7=r7c6=r9c9], r8c5, r9c4}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c6, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r4c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r4c9, r6c7, r6c6, r5c5} (source [r4c6=r6c3]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r4c6=r6c3]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c9, r8c7, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c7} and {r7c9, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c7) = X
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX
              box 5 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r5c5, r6c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c5) = X
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r4c9, r9c9‚âÝX

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r9c3=X ‚Üí [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, r7c6}: [r4c6=r5c8=r6c3], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X

              r6c6=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c6, r4c9, r5c8, r5c5}:
            Diagonals: {r4c6, r5c8} and {r4c9, r5c5} (non-adjacent).

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r4c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r4c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r4c6, [r5c5=r7c6], [r6c6=r8c5], r9c4} (spine [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r4c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c6=r8c5], r6c7, r8c7} (spine [r6c6=r8c5]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub r9c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r4c6=r9c4], r4c9, r7c9, [r6c3=r8c7], r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c7, r6c6, r8c5, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r5c5=r7c6] (edges r4c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r8c5‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr9c9), [r5c5=r7c6]‚Äîr7c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c5 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°
