      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r6c9=r7c8=r8c3], [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], r9c6} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c8=r7c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c9=r7c8=r8c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3=r9c6], r8c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r6c9=r7c8=r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r8c6], [r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3=r9c6], r8c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_guardian] (ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3‚Äîr9c2, r8c6‚Äîr9c6, r8c7‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2), r7c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r7c8, r8c7, r8c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r8c7} and {r7c8, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX
        r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9, r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c9=X
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
        r6c3=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r9c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c9=X
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
        r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r7c8=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r6c9=r7c8], r6c3, r7c3, r8c3} (spine r6c3‚Äîr7c3).
      ‚Üí color([r6c9=r7c8]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c6, [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3], r8c7, r9c9} (spine [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3]‚Äîr8c7).
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3], r9c2, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        r8c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)

        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c9=X
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        r6c3=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r9c6‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
        r7c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c7, r6c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c7‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c8, [r5c8=r6c3], r7c3, r8c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c3]‚Äîr7c3).
      ‚Üí color(r7c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], r7c3, [r7c8=r8c3], r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äî[r7c8=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c7, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], r6c9, r9c9} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c9, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c7=r9c9], [r7c8=r8c3]} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c7=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color([r7c8=r8c3]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r6c9=r7c8=r8c3], [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c7=r9c9], r9c6} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c7=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c9=r7c8=r8c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3=r9c6], r8c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r6c9=r7c8=r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r8c6], [r6c7=r9c9], [r6c9=r7c8=r8c3=r9c6], r8c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c9, r9c6} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r8c7, r7c8}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c7} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, [r6c9=r9c6]}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c7, [r6c9=r9c6]‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, [r6c9=r9c6]}: r6c7, [r6c9=r9c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2]=X
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r6c9=r9c6], r8c6}: [r6c9=r9c6], r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r9c6], r9c9}: [r6c9=r9c6], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, [r6c9=r9c6]}: r6c7=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c9=r9c6]‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c9=r9c6], r9c9}: [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c9=r9c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r9c6], r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, [r6c9=r9c6]}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X and r9c9=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c7‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], r9c6} (source r9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], r7c8, r8c7, r8c6, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c7], r3c6, r8c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, [r3c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c9=r9c8], r7c9} (spine [r3c9=r6c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c9=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r7c9], [r3c9=r6c6=r8c7], r6c3, r8c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c6=r8c7]‚Äîr6c3).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c9=r9c8], [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+hexagon+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c8} (source r9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 3, col 8}.
      Remainder: {r6c6, r9c2} = {r5c8, r8c3}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, [r6c8=r9c3], r6c6, r8c6, r8c7, r7c9}
      Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äî[r6c8=r9c3] ‚Üî r8c6‚Äîr8c7), r9c2‚Äîr9c8 (edges [r6c8=r9c3]‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c7‚Äîr7c9), r3c6‚Äîr3c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c6 ‚Üî r7c9‚Äîr5c9).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, r6c8, r9c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c9=r9c8], r9c2, r9c3, r8c3} (spine r9c2‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color([r5c9=r9c8]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]} and {r3c9, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]) = X
        row 8 {[r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c6, r8c7}: [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r9c2, r9c3}: [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c3‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c3, r9c3}: [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r6c8}: [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c9}: [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r7c9‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {[r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r8c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c9, r8c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r7c8‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {[r5c9=r7c8]}.
      ‚Üí color(r9c3) = color([r5c9=r7c8]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r6c3, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c3‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color([r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r6c6=r7c8=r9c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r8c7, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8], r9c9, r8c7} (spine [r5c9=r7c8]‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c7], r3c6, r8c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c9=r7c8], r6c8, r5c8} (spine [r5c9=r7c8]‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r6c6=r8c7]) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r6c3, r9c3, r9c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r9c3} and {r6c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7]) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r6c3, r6c8}: [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r8c5, r8c6}: [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c6‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r8c6}: [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c9=r7c8], r6c8}: [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7]=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r7c8]‚âÝX
        box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r6c3, r6c8}: r6c3=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r6c8‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c5‚âÝX
        col 8 {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c9=r7c8], r6c8}: [r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c9=r7c8]=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r8c7], [r5c9=r7c8], r9c9}: [r5c9=r7c8]=X and r9c9=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c8:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r6c8], r7c8, r8c7, r8c6}:

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r6c8]) = color(r8c7) = X

              r9c4=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)

              Contradiction.

              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

      ‚Üí color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c8, r6c6, r9c6, r9c7, r8c9}
      Bridges: r5c8‚Äî[r7c8=r9c3] (edges r5c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c7), r6c3‚Äî[r7c8=r9c3] (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c7‚Äîr8c9), r3c6‚Äîr3c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr9c6 ‚Üî r8c9‚Äîr5c9).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8], r8c9, r9c7} (spine [r5c9=r7c8]‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c7], r3c6, r9c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r9c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, [r3c9=r6c6=r9c7], [r5c9=r7c8], r8c9} (spine [r3c9=r6c6=r9c7]‚Äî[r5c9=r7c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r8c9], [r3c9=r6c6=r9c7], r6c3, r9c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c6=r9c7]‚Äîr6c3).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r6c8=r8c9=r9c3], r8c4, r8c5, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c9, r8c4, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r8c4‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c9, r3c9, r3c6, r6c6} (source [r8c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color([r8c9=r9c6]). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r8c9=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c8, r7c8, r9c7, r9c3, r6c3, r6c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_hexagon] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {[r5c9=r7c8]}.
      ‚Üí color(r9c3) = color([r5c9=r7c8]). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]} and {r3c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]) = X
        row 9 {[r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r9c6, r9c7}: [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r6c8}: [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r8c9}: [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r8c9‚âÝX
        [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 9 {[r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r9c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r8c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX
        box 8 {r8c4, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c5‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r5c9=r7c8=r9c3], r8c9, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c8} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8]} = {r5c8, r5c9, r8c3}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, [r6c6=r9c8], r9c3, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, r9c3} and {[r6c6=r9c8], r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8]‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r6c6=r9c8]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: [r6c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r6c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c8], r9c3}: [r6c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r6c6=r9c8], r7c6}: [r6c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r3c6, r7c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: [r6c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
        r5c9=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r5c9=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c8:

        2.  Guardian: r8c9 guards oddagon {r8c9, r3c9, r5c9, r5c8, r9c8} (source [r3c6=r6c8]).

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c8:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r6c8], r7c6, r7c7, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r6c8], r7c7} and {r7c6, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r6c8]) = color(r7c7) = X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r6c3, r6c6}: [r3c6=r6c8]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r6c6, r7c6}: [r3c6=r6c8]=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {[r3c6=r6c8], r5c8, r9c8}: [r3c6=r6c8]=X ‚Üí r5c8, r9c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r5c8, r5c9}: [r3c6=r6c8]=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX
              [r3c6=r6c8]=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí [r3c6=r6c8], r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {[r3c6=r6c8], r5c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r5c8, r5c9}: [r3c6=r6c8], r5c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r7c7, r8c9‚âÝX
              r5c9=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r6c3, r6c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c8). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r5c9} and {r3c9, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r5c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r6c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r8c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c8=X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r9c8‚âÝX

              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_set] (diamond+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8, [r6c3=r9c2]} (spine r6c8‚Äîr9c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c8, r6c6, r8c6, r8c7, r9c9}
      Bridges: [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r8c6‚Äîr8c7), [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr9c8 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c7‚Äîr9c9), r3c6‚Äîr3c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr5c9).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c7 guards oddagon {r6c7, r5c9, r3c9, r3c6, r6c6} (source [r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c8, r9c8, r8c7, r8c3, r6c3, r6c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c3, r9c8} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c9, [r8c3=r9c8], r8c7, r8c6} (spine [r8c3=r9c8]‚Äîr8c7).
      ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c7, [r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r5c9} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äî[r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c6], [r3c6=r5c9], [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r5c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c9]‚Äî[r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r6c6]) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6], [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], [r8c3=r9c8]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3, r9c8} (spine r9c2‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, [r8c3=r9c8], r8c7, r8c6} (spine [r8c3=r9c8]‚Äîr8c7).
      ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r6c7 guards oddagon {r6c7, r5c9, r3c9, r3c6, r6c6} (source [r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c8, [r8c3=r9c8], r6c3, r6c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c7, [r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r9c2, r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c3, [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]}: [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r6c6], r6c3‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c8}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c8‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 8 {[r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6], r8c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c3‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
        r9c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c3, [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]}: r6c3, [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]‚âÝX ‚Üí [r3c9=r6c6]=X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]}: [r3c9=r6c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]}: [r3c9=r6c6]=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r3c9=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, [r5c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c6], r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, [r7c9=r8c6]} and {[r5c9=r6c3], r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color([r7c9=r8c6]) = X
        row 8 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c7}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6]‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r6c3]‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c7, r9c8}: [r7c9=r8c6]=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r5c9=r6c3]) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r6c6], r6c7‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c6]}: [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí [r7c9=r8c6]‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        box 9 {[r7c9=r8c6], r8c7, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
        [r5c9=r6c3]=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r9c7], r6c7, r5c8, r7c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r9c7], r5c8} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r9c7]) = color(r5c8) = X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c7], r9c3, r9c6}: [r3c6=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r9c7], r6c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c9, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r3c6=r9c7], r7c8, r8c9}: [r3c6=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r7c8, r8c9‚âÝX
              [r3c6=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX

              col 6 {[r3c6=r9c7], r6c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r9c7], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              box 8 {r8c4, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c5‚âÝX
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c4, r8c5, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c7, r9c7, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c7} and {r6c7, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c7) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX

              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí r7c8, r8c9‚âÝX
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r3c6=X and r9c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c9, r8c4, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r8c4‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r8c9=r9c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r8c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r3c9=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äîr6c7).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, [r5c9=r6c3], [r8c9=r9c6], r7c8}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, [r8c9=r9c6]} and {[r5c9=r6c3], r7c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color([r8c9=r9c6]) = X
        row 8 {r8c4, r8c5, [r8c9=r9c6]}: [r8c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c5‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r8c9=r9c6], r9c3, r9c7}: [r8c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r8c9=r9c6]}: [r8c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6]‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], [r8c9=r9c6]}: [r8c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r6c3]‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, [r8c9=r9c6], r9c7}: [r8c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r5c9=r6c3]) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r6c6], r6c7‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3], [r8c9=r9c6]}: [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí [r8c9=r9c6]‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r6c3], r6c7}: [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, [r8c9=r9c6], r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
        [r5c9=r6c3]=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 9 {[r8c9=r9c6], r9c3, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c9, r8c4, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r8c4‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r8c9=r9c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r8c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.

      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r8c9=r9c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r8c9=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, r5c8, r7c8, r9c7, r9c3, r6c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+hexagon+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c8=r8c5], r6c7, r8c7} (spine [r5c8=r8c5]‚Äîr6c7).
            ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c7, [r5c8=r8c5], [r5c9=r8c7], r7c8} (spine [r5c8=r8c5]‚Äî[r5c9=r8c7]).
            ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r7c8). Identify.
        4.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c6, [r6c7=r7c8] (also r6c3)
            row 9: r9c5, r9c9 (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, [r6c7=r7c8]
            row 8: [r5c8=r8c5], [r5c9=r8c7]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c8, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r9c5).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c5 (col 5) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c8=r8c5], [r6c6=r9c5], [r6c7=r7c8], r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c7, r6c6, r7c6, r9c5, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr8c5 (edges r5c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c5), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c5‚Äîr9c9), r3c6‚Äîr3c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr5c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r6c6:

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c7) = X

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X

              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r8c7, r9c9‚âÝX

              Contradiction.

            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c7) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, [r5c8=r6c6]‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c8=r6c6], r6c3, r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              box 6 {[r5c8=r6c6], r5c9, r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              r5c9=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c4‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r8c7, r9c9‚âÝX
              r7c8=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r6c6]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r7c6}: [r5c8=r6c6], r7c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c8=r6c6], r6c3, r6c7}: [r5c8=r6c6], r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              box 6 {[r5c8=r6c6], r5c9, r6c7}: r5c9=X and r6c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r6c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r8c7, r7c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c7} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7) = X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c9, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X and r9c4=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX

              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r8c7:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        2.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c7, r6c6, r7c6, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: [r5c8=r8c7]‚Äîr8c5 (edges r5c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr9c9), r3c6‚Äîr3c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr5c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c7). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r6c7, r5c8, r8c7, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr8c7).
            ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r7c8). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9, [r6c7=r7c8]} (spine r5c9‚Äîr9c9).
            ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r6c7=r7c8]). Identify.
        5.  K‚ÇÑ on {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c7=r7c8], r6c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, r9c6} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c7=r9c6] (also r6c6)
      row 9: [r6c3=r9c2], r9c8 (also [r6c7=r9c6])
      row 7: r7c3, r7c9
      row 8: r8c3, r8c7
      Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c2, r7c3, r8c3} in box 7, {r9c8, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 6 and 9: r6c7‚Üîr9c8 (box 6), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing [r6c3=r9c2] and [r6c3=r9c2]).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr8: r6c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r6‚Üîr9: r6c7‚Üîr9c8 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, [r6c7=r9c6], r9c8} (spine r5c8‚Äî[r6c7=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c9=r9c8], [r6c7=r9c6], r8c7} (spine [r5c9=r9c8]‚Äî[r6c7=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r6c7=r9c6], [r5c8=r8c7], [r5c9=r9c8], r7c9} (spine [r5c8=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c9=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c7=r9c6]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r6c7=r7c9=r9c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r6c7=r7c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r6c7=r7c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r9c8]} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äî[r6c7=r7c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r5c9=r9c8]). Identify.

      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r6c6]) = color([r5c8=r8c7]). Identify.
      (proof search failed ‚Äî depth limit reached)

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r8c7:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.

              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r5c9, r7c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X

              Contradiction.

            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond+xwing+parity+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r9c1:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c1], r3c6, r9c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr9c6).
            ‚Üí color([r3c9=r9c1]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        3.  Gradient chain (with identification):
            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c3, r6c7 (also [r3c9=r6c6=r9c1])
            row 9: [r3c9=r6c6=r9c1], r9c8 (also r9c6)
            row 7: r7c3, r7c9
            row 8: r8c2, r8c7
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c1, r7c3, r8c2} in box 7, {r9c8, r7c9, r8c7} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4), r6c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c7 and r9c8).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c3‚Üîr7c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c7‚Üîr8c7 (col 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c3‚Üîr8c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c3‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c2‚Üîr9c1 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c7) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        4.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r6c6=r9c1], r5c9, [r6c7=r9c8], r7c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c1). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r8c7, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c7} and {r6c7, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c7) = X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c9, r6c7‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c8‚âÝX
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r5c9, r7c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6, r9c1‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c1, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c1}: r8c2, r9c1‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c3=X
              r7c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c6‚âÝX

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===

            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c9‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c5=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r8c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r5c9, r8c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9, r9c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
              r7c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r8c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r8c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c5], r3c9, r8c9, r5c9} (spine r3c9‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r8c5]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c6=r5c9=r8c5], r6c5, r6c8, r6c3} (spine r6c5‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r5c9=r8c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c9, r9c7, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r9c7} and {r8c9, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5]) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r6c5, r6c8}: [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c6, r8c9}: [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r3c9, r8c9}: [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r5c8, r6c8}: [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r9c7=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 8 {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c6, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c6‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r5c9=r6c3=r8c5], r8c6, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r8c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c5], r3c9, r8c9, r5c9} (spine r3c9‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r8c5]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c8}.
      ‚Üí color(r9c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c8 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r5c9=r8c5], r5c8, [r7c8=r9c3], r6c3, r6c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.

  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c7], r3c6, r9c6, r7c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r9c7]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r8c9, [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7], r8c5, r9c6} (spine [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7]‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7], [r8c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r7c8]} (spine [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7]‚Äî[r8c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r5c9=r7c8]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c6=r5c9=r7c8], [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7], [r8c9=r9c6], r8c5} (spine [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7]‚Äî[r8c9=r9c6]).

      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r3c6=r5c9=r7c8=r8c5], [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7], [r8c9=r9c6], r9c3} (spine [r3c9=r7c6=r9c7]‚Äî[r8c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r5c9=r7c8=r8c5]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c6=r5c9=r7c8=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c8=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  K‚ÇÑ on {r3c6, [r3c9=r8c5], r7c6, r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r8c5, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r8c5} and {r3c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r8c5) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r7c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c5=X ‚Üí r6c5, r8c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r9c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r7c6‚âÝX

              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6‚Äîr9c6).

      ‚Üí color(r7c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r7c6=r9c8], r7c8, r7c9} (spine [r7c6=r9c8]‚Äîr7c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c8=r7c9], r5c9, r6c7} (spine [r5c8=r7c9]‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c5], [r3c9=r6c7], r6c5, r6c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c7]‚Äîr6c5).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r8c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c6=r6c3=r8c5], [r7c6=r9c8], r9c3, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (SET) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 7, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r8c5}.
      ‚Üí color(r9c3) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r6c7}.
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  3.  SET: {col 6, col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c7]} = {[r3c9=r6c7], [r8c5=r9c3]}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r6c7], r6c3, r6c5, [r8c5=r9c3]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c6, r7c8, r7c9, r9c8} (spine r7c8‚Äîr7c9).
      ‚Üí color(r7c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c5, [r7c6=r9c8], r9c6, r9c3} (spine [r7c6=r9c8]‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c7, r6c3, r6c5, [r8c5=r9c3]} (spine r6c3‚Äîr6c5).
      ‚Üí color(r6c7) = color([r8c5=r9c3]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, [r7c6=r9c8]}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r7c9} and {r3c9, [r7c6=r9c8]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r9c8], r7c8, r7c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí [r7c6=r9c8], r7c8‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r7c6=r9c8], r9c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r7c6=r9c8], r7c8}: [r7c6=r9c8], r7c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r5c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, [r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]}: r5c8=X ‚Üí [r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], [r7c6=r9c8], r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color([r7c6=r9c8]) = X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r9c8], r7c8, r7c9}: [r7c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r7c8, r7c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], [r7c6=r9c8], r9c6}: [r7c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí [r6c7=r8c5=r9c3], r9c6‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r7c6=r9c8], r9c6}: [r7c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, [r7c6=r9c8], r7c8}: [r7c6=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, [r6c7=r8c5=r9c3]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===

            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c8, r6c9‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6, r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c3, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c4=X
              box 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r9c4}: r9c4=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c6‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c5, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c8‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r6c8, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r7c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c8=X and r6c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], r9c3, r9c8, r9c4} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c8).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]} (spine r5c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r3c9, r7c9, r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r7c9} and {r3c9, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r6c8, r6c9}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]=X ‚Üí r6c8, r6c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r9c3, r9c8}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c8‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r5c6, r8c6}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r8c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r8c5, r8c6}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4]=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c5, r8c6, r8c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 9 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r9c3, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r9c3‚âÝX
        col 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r6c9, r7c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r6c8, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], r9c3, r9c8, r9c4} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c8).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  SET: {col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r8c7}.
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r8c6 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, [r3c9=r8c7], r8c5, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c4], r5c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c8, r6c9‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r3c9=r8c7], r8c3, r8c8}: [r3c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r8c7], r6c9, r7c9}: [r3c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
        [r3c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
        r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r3c9=r8c7], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c3=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r8c7], r8c8‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r8c7], r6c9, r7c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r7c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c8=X and r6c9=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c3, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, [r6c8=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8], r6c3} (spine [r6c8=r8c3]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], [r6c8=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c7} (spine [r6c8=r8c3]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r8c3], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r8c8], r7c9} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r8c3]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]} (spine [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], r3c6, r5c6, r9c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r8c7=r9c6], [r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c6} (spine r9c1‚Äîr9c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c7, [r8c3=r9c6]} (source [r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r6c9=r8c8]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r6c9=r8c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r6c8, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c6]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3, r8c7} (spine [r6c9=r8c8]‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c7], r8c3} = {r3c9, r5c8, r7c9}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  K‚ÇÑ on {r3c9, r5c8, [r6c9=r8c8], r7c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing) ===

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 9}.

      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.

        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        box 9 {[r3c9=r8c7], r7c8, r9c9}: [r3c9=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX

      Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r3c9=r8c7], r8c3, r8c6}: r8c3=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r8c7], r8c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r8c7], r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
        box 9 {[r3c9=r8c7], r7c8, r9c9}: [r3c9=r8c7], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {[r6c9=r7c8]}.
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color([r6c9=r7c8]). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c9=r7c8=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r8c7, r8c3, r6c3, r6c8, r5c8, r5c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, [r6c9=r7c8], r8c7, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r8c7} and {[r6c9=r7c8], r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r8c7) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8]}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8]‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8]}: r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        box 9 {[r6c9=r7c8], r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
        r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9}: [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r6c9=r7c8]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8]}: [r6c9=r7c8]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c7‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8]}: [r6c9=r7c8]=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9}: [r6c9=r7c8]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8], r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äî[r6c9=r7c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c8], r9c9, r8c7} (spine [r6c9=r7c8]‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c7], r3c6, r8c6, r5c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c7]) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c9=r7c8], [r3c9=r5c6=r8c7], [r5c8=r6c3], r8c3} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c8=r6c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c9=r7c8]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r6c9=r7c8=r8c3], r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing) ===

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 9}.

      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5, r8c9} (spine r8c4‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r7c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c3, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r6c9=r8c8], r6c3, [r6c8=r8c3], r9c3} (spine r6c3‚Äî[r6c8=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c9=r8c8]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c8, [r6c8=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8=r9c3], r6c3} (spine [r6c8=r8c3]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], [r6c8=r8c3], [r6c9=r8c8=r9c3], r8c7} (spine [r6c8=r8c3]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r6c8=r8c3], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r8c8=r9c3], r9c9} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r8c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9], [r6c9=r8c8=r9c3], [r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]} (spine [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9]‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r8c7], r3c6, r5c6, r7c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr5c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r8c7]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r7c6=r8c7], [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9], [r6c9=r8c8=r9c3], r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r6c3‚Äî[r6c9=r8c8], r8c3‚Äîr8c7, r9c3‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3, r8c7} (spine [r6c9=r8c8]‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r8c7], r6c8, r9c5, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c3=r8c7], r9c5} and {r6c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c3=r8c7]) = color(r9c5) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r8c7], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c7]=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r8c8], r9c9}: [r6c9=r8c8], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r9c5=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r8c7], r6c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}: r6c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r8c8]‚âÝX
        row 8 {[r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c3=r8c7], [r6c9=r8c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c5, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r8c7], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X and r9c3=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        row 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
        col 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r5c6, r7c6}: [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r7c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí [r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r8c3‚âÝX
        col 8 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r6c9, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {[r3c6=r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r6c8, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], r8c3, r8c8, r8c5} (spine r8c3‚Äîr8c8).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r8c3} = {r3c9, [r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r9c9}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  K‚ÇÑ on {r3c9, [r5c8=r6c3=r8c5], r6c9, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c8, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], r6c9, r9c9} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr6c9).

  4.  Diamond {r6c9, [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c8=r9c9], [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], [r6c9=r9c3], r8c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]‚Äî[r6c9=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9], [r6c9=r9c3], [r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]} (spine [r6c8=r8c3=r9c9]‚Äî[r6c9=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r5c8=r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r9c2], r3c6, r5c6, r7c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr5c6).

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9, r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äîr6c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5, r8c3} (spine r8c4‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c9} (spine r9c2‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color([r7c6=r8c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c6=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, [r5c8=r6c3], r5c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c9, r6c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r6c3} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c8, r6c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r8c3, r9c3‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c7=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c3} = {r3c9, r5c8, [r6c9=r8c8]}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c9=r8c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c9, r5c8, r6c8, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r7c7], r3c6, r7c6, r5c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr7c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r7c7]) = color(r5c6). Identify.

  7.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c8=r6c3=r9c9], [r6c8=r8c3], r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c8, [r6c9=r7c8], r6c3} (spine r6c8‚Äî[r6c9=r7c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c6, [r6c9=r7c8], r7c7, r9c9} (spine [r6c9=r7c8]‚Äîr7c7).
      ‚Üí color(r7c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r7c6=r9c9], r5c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r7c6=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c3], [r3c9=r5c6], [r6c9=r7c8], [r7c6=r9c9]} (spine [r3c9=r5c6]‚Äî[r6c9=r7c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c8=r6c3]) = color([r7c6=r9c9]). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c8=r6c3=r7c6=r9c9], r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c6, r7c7, r7c8, r9c9} (spine r7c7‚Äîr7c8).
      ‚Üí color(r7c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r7c6=r9c9], r5c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r7c6=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c8, r7c8, [r7c6=r9c9]}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r5c6], r7c8} and {r5c8, [r7c6=r9c9]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r5c6]) = color(r7c8) = X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r9c9], r7c7, r7c8}: r7c8=X ‚Üí [r7c6=r9c9], r7c7‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c6], [r7c6=r9c9]}: [r3c9=r5c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r7c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c6], r6c9, [r7c6=r9c9]}: [r3c9=r5c6]=X ‚Üí r6c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color([r7c6=r9c9]) = X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r9c9], r7c7, r7c8}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r7c7, r7c8‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r7c6=r9c9], r9c1, r9c2}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c6], [r7c6=r9c9]}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r3c6, [r3c9=r5c6]‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r5c6], r6c9, [r7c6=r9c9]}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X ‚Üí r6c9‚âÝX
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
        r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r6c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c9} (spine r9c1‚Äîr9c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, [r8c3=r9c9], r7c7, r7c6} (source [r6c9=r7c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r6c9=r7c8]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r6c9=r7c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r6c8, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c9], r7c7, r7c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9‚âÝX ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2]=X
