Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c6, [r6c3=r9c2]} = {r3c9, r6c8}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c8, r9c8, [r6c3=r9c2]}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r9c8} and {r5c8, [r6c3=r9c2]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r9c9‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r8c6‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r9c2]) = X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c8, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c8‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        box 9 {r8c7, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
        r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c6=r6c8], r5c8, r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c8]‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c9=r9c8], r9c9, r8c7} (spine [r5c9=r9c8]‚Äîr9c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c7], r3c6, r8c6, [r5c6=r6c8]} (spine r3c6‚Äîr8c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c7]) = color([r5c6=r6c8]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c8, [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7], [r5c9=r9c8], r9c9} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7]‚Äî[r5c9=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r6c3, [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7], r8c3, r8c6} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7]‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7], [r6c3=r8c6], r8c3} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7]‚Äî[r6c3=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7], [r3c6=r8c3], [r6c3=r8c6], r7c3} (spine [r3c6=r8c3]‚Äî[r6c3=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r8c7]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  9.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r7c3=r8c7], [r5c8=r9c9], [r5c9=r9c8], r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c3}.
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r8c7}.
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  SET: {col 3, col 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c8} = {r5c9, r9c2}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c6, r5c9, r9c9, r9c8, r6c3, [r7c3=r8c6]}
      Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr6c8 (edges r5c6‚Äîr5c9 ‚Üî r9c8‚Äîr6c3), [r3c9=r8c7]‚Äîr8c3 (edges r5c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äî[r7c3=r8c6]), [r3c9=r8c7]‚Äîr3c6 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c8 ‚Üî [r7c3=r8c6]‚Äîr5c6).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c8], [r6c3=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]‚Äîr9c8).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c8]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c8, r9c8, r8c7, r8c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, r6c3} (spine r6c4‚Äîr6c5).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, [r5c6=r6c3], r9c3, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c3]‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c6], r9c3} (spine [r5c6=r6c3]‚Äî[r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c3], [r3c6=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c6], r9c9} (spine [r3c6=r9c3]‚Äî[r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c3], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c9], [r8c3=r9c6]} (spine [r3c6=r9c3]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r8c3=r9c6]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c3], [r3c9=r8c3=r9c6], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c9], r5c9} (spine [r3c9=r8c3=r9c6]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r9c3]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c6], [r3c6=r5c9=r9c3], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c9], r5c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c9=r9c3]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c3=r9c6]) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r8c3=r9c6], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c9], r7c8, r8c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, r6c3} (spine r6c4‚Äîr6c5).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, [r5c6=r6c3], r9c3, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c3]‚Äîr9c3).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, [r8c3=r9c6]}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r9c9} and {r3c9, [r8c3=r9c6]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 9 {[r8c3=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r8c3=r9c6], r9c3‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c6], r9c3}: [r8c3=r9c6], r9c3‚âÝX ‚Üí [r5c6=r6c3]=X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c6]}: r3c6=X and [r5c6=r6c3]=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color([r8c3=r9c6]) = X
        row 9 {[r8c3=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: [r8c3=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r5c6=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c6], r9c3}: [r8c3=r9c6]=X ‚Üí [r5c6=r6c3]‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r5c6=r6c3], [r8c3=r9c6]}: [r8c3=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
        [r8c3=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 5 {[r5c6=r6c3], r5c8, r5c9}: [r5c6=r6c3], r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c8=X
        r5c8=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r7c9, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r7c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c6, r5c7, r6c9, [r7c9=r9c3], r8c3, r8c6}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr8c8 (edges r5c6‚Äîr5c7 ‚Üî [r7c9=r9c3]‚Äîr8c3), r5c8‚Äîr8c8 (edges r5c7‚Äîr6c9 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr8c6), r3c9‚Äîr3c6 (edges r6c9‚Äî[r7c9=r9c3] ‚Üî r8c6‚Äîr5c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c9, r7c9, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r7c9} and {r6c9, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r7c9) = X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r8c3, r9c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r6c9‚âÝX
              r7c9=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX
              box 8 {r8c6, r9c4, r9c5}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c5‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r7c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
              box 8 {r8c6, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c4, r9c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c6=X
              r8c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c6, r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

              row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c8}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c8‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r8c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r7c6}: r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r7c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c3, r8c7, r8c8, r9c9} (spine r8c7‚Äîr8c8).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9], r6c3, r9c3, r9c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).

      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c9, r8c5, r9c6}:

        Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c9, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3‚Äîr8c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r5c6, [r6c3=r9c2]} = {r6c9, r8c5}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c9, r8c5, r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c3=r9c2], r8c5} and {r6c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c5) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c9‚âÝX
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c4, r9c6}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c6‚âÝX
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
        [r6c3=r9c2]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r6c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
        r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c4, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí [r6c3=r9c2], r9c4‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r8c9‚âÝX
        box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
        box 8 {r8c5, r9c4, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r5c7}.
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r5c7). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r8c8 guards oddagon {r8c8, r5c8, [r3c9=r5c7], r3c6, r8c6} (source r5c6).
      ‚Üí color(r8c8) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r8c8], r8c3, r9c2, r9c8}:

  9.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c9=r8c8], [r3c6=r5c7=r7c9=r8c3], [r3c9=r5c8=r8c6=r9c7], r9c2} (spine [r3c6=r5c7=r7c9=r8c3]‚Äî[r3c9=r5c8=r8c6=r9c7]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c9=r8c8]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  10.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c6=r5c7=r7c9=r8c3], [r5c6=r6c9=r8c8=r9c2], r6c3, r7c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r9c7:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r9c7], r8c8, r8c3, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c9=r9c7], r8c3} and {r8c8, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c9=r9c7]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r7c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], r6c9, r7c9}: [r3c9=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r6c9, r7c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              [r3c9=r9c7]=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r3c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c6=X
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r5c7, r6c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r9c7], r7c9‚âÝX
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], r6c9, r7c9}: [r3c9=r9c7], r7c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c9=X
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c7‚âÝX
              r6c3=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c7). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c7, r5c8, r8c8, r9c7}:
            Diagonals: {r5c7, r8c8} and {r5c8, r9c7} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c7) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c7=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r5c7=X ‚Üí r6c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c7‚âÝX
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r9c7) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c7‚âÝX

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).

  3.  Diamond {r7c9, [r3c9=r8c6], r8c8, r8c3} (spine [r3c9=r8c6]‚Äîr8c8).
      ‚Üí color(r7c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c9=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c9=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c9], r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c7, r8c8} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).

        row 8 {[r3c9=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r5c6=r6c9=r8c8]=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r8c6], r8c3‚âÝX

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).

  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r7c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r7c6], r8c5, r8c9, r8c3} (spine r8c5‚Äîr8c9).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r7c6]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r7c6=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c6=r6c9], r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c5, r8c5, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r7c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c9, [r3c9=r7c6], r8c5, r9c5} (spine [r3c9=r7c6]‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r6c9], r6c3, r8c3, [r8c9=r9c5]}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r6c9], r8c3} and {r6c3, [r8c9=r9c5]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c9]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 5 {[r5c6=r6c9], r5c7, r5c8}: [r5c6=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, [r8c9=r9c5]}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, [r8c9=r9c5]‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r7c3‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9]}: [r5c6=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6]‚âÝX
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r3c9=r7c6], r8c5, [r8c9=r9c5]}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c3) = color([r8c9=r9c5]) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, [r8c9=r9c5]}: [r8c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c5‚âÝX
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9], [r8c9=r9c5]}: [r8c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9]‚âÝX
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
        [r8c9=r9c5]=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r7c6], [r3c6=r9c9], [r5c6=r6c9], [r6c3=r9c2]} (spine [r3c6=r9c9]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r7c6]) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r6c3=r7c6=r9c2], r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r5c6, r8c3} = {r6c9, r7c6}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r8c3, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c9} and {r3c9, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r7c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r5c6, r7c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, [r8c3=r9c9]}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, [r8c3=r9c9]‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
              r6c9=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c6) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, [r8c3=r9c9]}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r6c9, [r8c3=r9c9]‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r7c6=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c5‚âÝX
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r7c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {[r8c3=r9c9], r8c4, r8c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r8c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c9, r9c9, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c9} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r6c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              r9c2=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c4, r8c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r7c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              r7c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c9=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.

      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 5, row 8}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r5c6=r6c9], r8c3} = {[r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9]}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r6c9], r8c3, r9c2, r9c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r5c6=r6c9], r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r5c6=r6c9]) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 5 {[r5c6=r6c9], r5c7, r5c8}: [r5c6=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9]}: [r5c6=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6]‚âÝX
        col 9 {[r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9], r9c9}: [r5c6=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r7c3, r8c3‚âÝX
        [r5c6=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X

        col 9 {[r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9], r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c9]‚âÝX

        box 8 {[r3c9=r7c6], r8c4, r8c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r9c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c6=r6c9], r8c9} (spine [r3c9=r9c6]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c9], [r3c6=r8c9], [r3c9=r9c6], r8c5} (spine [r3c6=r8c9]‚Äî[r3c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c9]) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c9=r8c5], [r3c6=r8c9], [r3c9=r9c6], r9c8} (spine [r3c6=r8c9]‚Äî[r3c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r6c9=r8c5]) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c9], [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c6=r6c9=r8c5=r9c8], r7c5} (spine [r3c9=r9c6]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9=r8c5=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r8c9]) = color(r7c5). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], [r3c6=r7c5=r8c9], [r5c6=r6c9=r8c5=r9c8], r6c5} (spine [r3c6=r7c5=r8c9]‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9=r8c5=r9c8]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r6c5=r9c6], [r5c6=r6c9=r8c5=r9c8], r6c3, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r8c9:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r5c8=r8c9], r5c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, [r5c8=r8c9]} and {r3c9, r5c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r5c8=r8c9]) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, [r5c8=r8c9]}: [r5c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c7‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c9}: [r5c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r6c9‚âÝX
              [r5c8=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c5=X
              r7c5=X ‚Üí r6c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r5c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, [r5c8=r8c9]}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r5c7, [r5c8=r8c9]‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r6c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c7, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c9). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c9, r6c5, r7c5, r9c6, r9c8}
            Bridges: r8c9‚Äîr8c5 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c9 ‚Üî r7c5‚Äîr9c6), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c9‚Äîr6c5 ‚Üî r9c6‚Äîr9c8), r8c5‚Äîr8c9 (edges r6c5‚Äîr7c5 ‚Üî r9c8‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c9], r9c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r5c6=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], r7c5, r8c5, r6c5} (spine r7c5‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r6c5=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c6=r6c9], r6c3, r9c3, r9c8, r8c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r6c5], r7c5, r8c4, r8c9}:

              r8c9=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX (adjacent)

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r6c5, r5c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c5} and {r3c9, r5c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r6c3‚Äîr8c3, r6c6‚Äîr8c6, r6c8‚Äîr8c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2), r5c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Branch on r5c8, r8c6:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r6c6, r5c5, r9c5, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r8c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c6, r8c8, r9c9, r9c5}:

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r9c5:

            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

            row 6: r6c6, r6c8 (also r6c3)

            row 8: r8c5, r8c8
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c5, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.

              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c8 and r9c9).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.

                r6‚Üîr7: r6c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.

            For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
            Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
            row 6: r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9] (also r6c3)
            row 9: r9c4, [r5c8=r9c9] (also r9c3)
            row 7: r7c6, [r6c8=r7c9]
            row 8: r8c5, [r3c9=r8c8]
            Rows 9, 7, 8: {r9c4, r7c6, r8c5} in box 8, {r9c9, r7c9, r8c8} in box 9 ‚Äî circular ladder of length 3 ‚Üí same Œ¥.
            Rows 6 and 9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6), r6c3‚Üîr9c3 (col 3) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
              Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r6c6 and r9c4).
            Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
              row 6, row 7, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
                r6‚Üîr7: r6c6‚Üîr7c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr8: r6c8‚Üîr8c8 (col 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r6‚Üîr9: r6c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr8: r7c6‚Üîr8c5 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r7‚Üîr9: r7c6‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
                r8‚Üîr9: r8c5‚Üîr9c4 (box 8) ‚Üí pairs differ.
              4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
            Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r6c6) = color(r9c4). Identify.
        5.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c5, [r5c8=r9c9], [r6c8=r7c9], r7c6, r8c5} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        6.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c8, r6c8, r6c6, r7c6, r9c4, r9c9}
            Bridges: [r3c9=r8c8]‚Äîr8c5 (edges r5c8‚Äîr6c8 ‚Üî r7c6‚Äîr9c4), r6c3‚Äîr9c3 (edges r6c8‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r9c4‚Äîr9c9), r3c6‚Äî[r3c9=r8c8] (edges r6c6‚Äîr7c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr5c8).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3‚Äîr9c3, r8c6‚Äîr9c5, r8c8‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r7c9, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r7c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r8c8, r8c6} (source [r7c9=r9c5]).
            ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r7c9=r9c5]). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r5c5, r5c8, r8c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r5c8} and {r5c5, r8c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r7c9=r9c5]) = color(r5c8) = X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r9c3, r9c9}: [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r6c6, r8c6}: [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r3c9, r9c9}: [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r8c8, r9c9}: [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX
              [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r5c5, r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c5) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X ‚Üí [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r9c9‚âÝX
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r5c8, r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r9c3, r9c9}: [r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r7c9=r9c5], r6c6, r8c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c9) ‚âÝ color(r9c5). Add edge.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r7c9, r8c8, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c6, r6c7‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r7c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c5=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r7c9, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c6, r8c8‚âÝX
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r9c3‚âÝX

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r6c5, r6c6, r6c3} (spine r6c5‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8, r9c9} (spine r7c8‚Äîr8c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c5=r6c3], [r5c8=r9c9], r9c3} (spine [r5c5=r6c3]‚Äî[r5c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c9) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c8=r9c9], [r5c9=r9c3], [r5c5=r6c3]} (spine [r5c8=r9c9]‚Äî[r5c9=r9c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r5c5=r6c3]). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c5=r6c3], r3c6, r6c6, r8c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr6c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r5c5=r6c3]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c5=r6c3=r8c6], [r5c8=r9c9], [r5c9=r9c3], r9c4}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8, r9c9} (spine r7c8‚Äîr8c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c8=r9c9], r5c9, r5c5} (spine [r5c8=r9c9]‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r5c5). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r9c4 guards oddagon {r9c4, r6c4, [r3c9=r5c5], r3c6, r8c6} (source r6c6).
      ‚Üí color(r9c4) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c9=r5c5], [r5c8=r9c9], r9c3, r6c3, r6c4} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r6c4:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r6c4). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c6, r8c8, r9c9, r9c4}:
            Diagonals: {r8c6, r9c9} and {r8c8, r9c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c6) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c4‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r6c4], r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, [r5c9=r6c4]‚âÝX
              box 5 {r5c5, [r5c9=r6c4], r6c6}: [r5c9=r6c4], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c9=r6c4], r6c3, r6c6}: [r5c9=r6c4], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 8 {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X ‚Üí r5c8, r7c8‚âÝX
              r8c8=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c4=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r6c4]‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c8, [r5c9=r6c4]}: r5c8, [r5c9=r6c4]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r6c4], r9c9}: [r5c9=r6c4], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              box 5 {r5c5, [r5c9=r6c4], r6c6}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c9=r6c4], r6c3, r6c6}: [r5c9=r6c4], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) ‚âÝ color(r6c4). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c4, r9c4, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c4} and {r6c4, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c6}: r6c3=X ‚Üí r6c4, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r5c5, r6c4, r6c6}: r6c4, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              r5c5=X ‚Üí r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c4=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c6=X and r3c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c4) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c6}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c4, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 5 {r5c5, r6c4, r6c6}: r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c5‚âÝX
              r6c4=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c5, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r7c8, r8c8‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c8, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c9} (spine r9c1‚Äîr9c2).

  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, [r8c3=r9c9]} (spine [r5c5=r6c9]‚Äîr6c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color([r8c3=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r6c6=r8c3=r9c9], r8c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r6c6=r8c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  5.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r8c6], [r5c5=r6c9], [r6c6=r8c3=r9c9], r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c5, r6c9:
      Case A: color(r5c5) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, r8c3, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c5=r6c9], r8c3} and {r6c3, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c5=r6c9]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 5 {[r5c5=r6c9], r5c7, r5c8}: [r5c5=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {[r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6}: [r5c5=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c6}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9], r9c9}: [r5c5=r6c9]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c3) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 6 {[r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6}: r6c3=X ‚Üí [r5c5=r6c9], r6c6‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c6}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9], r9c9}: r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9]‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r9c1, r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c5) ‚âÝ color(r6c9). Add edge.
        3.  K‚ÇÑ on {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c9} (spine r9c1‚Äîr9c2).
      ‚Üí color(r8c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, [r8c3=r9c9]} (spine [r5c5=r6c9]‚Äîr6c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color([r8c3=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9], r8c5, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

        [r7c5=r8c9]=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6}: [r5c5=r6c9], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6, r9c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
        r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c8‚âÝX
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
        col 9 {r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9], [r7c5=r8c9]}: r3c9=X ‚Üí [r5c5=r6c9], [r7c5=r8c9]‚âÝX
        box 8 {[r7c5=r8c9], r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX
        row 6 {[r5c5=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6}: [r5c5=r6c9], r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
        col 5 {[r5c5=r6c9], [r7c5=r8c9], r8c5}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c5, [r5c5=r6c9], r8c5, r8c9} (spine [r5c5=r6c9]‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color(r7c5) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c5=r6c9], [r7c5=r8c9], r8c5} (spine [r5c5=r6c9]‚Äî[r7c5=r8c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c5], r3c6, r9c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c5]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c3, [r3c9=r6c6=r8c5], [r5c5=r6c9], [r7c5=r8c9]} (spine [r3c9=r6c6=r8c5]‚Äî[r5c5=r6c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color([r7c5=r8c9]). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r6c3=r7c5=r8c9], r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r6c6:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c9, r8c9, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r8c9} and {r6c9, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r8c9) = X
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c5, r5c7‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r6c6], r6c9‚âÝX
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r9c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c9=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c3, r6c9}: [r3c9=r6c6], r6c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 5 {r5c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r5c5, r8c5‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c5=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c5=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              r6c3=X ‚Üí r9c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c3, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r6c6], r6c3‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], r9c6}: [r3c9=r6c6], r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c6=X
              col 9 {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c7, r5c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c5=X
              col 5 {r5c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r5c5=X ‚Üí r7c5, r8c5‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) ‚âÝ color(r6c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c5, r8c9, r9c8, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r8c5, r9c8} and {r8c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c5) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 5 {r5c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí r5c5, r7c5‚âÝX
              r8c5=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c8=X ‚Üí r5c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c5, r5c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c7=X
              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: r5c7=X ‚Üí r6c9‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9, r8c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c6, r6c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c8‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r6c9‚âÝX
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r7c5, r8c5‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c6, r6c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c3=X

              box 6 {r5c7, r5c8, r6c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c7, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r5c7‚Äîr5c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c5) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c5=r6c9], r7c5, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r7c5‚Äîr8c5).
      ‚Üí color([r5c5=r6c9]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c5=r6c9=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r8c9, r9c8, r9c3, r6c3, r6c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+hexagon+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r5c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r5c9). Identify.
        2.  Circular ladder {[r3c6=r5c9]‚Äîr6c6, r5c3‚Äîr6c3, r5c8‚Äîr6c8}:
            Satellites r9c6 (rung 1), r8c3 (rung 2), r9c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
        3.  K‚ÇÑ on {r8c3, r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) ‚âÝ color(r5c9). Add edge.
        4.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r8c9, r8c3, r6c3, r6c6}
            Bridges: r5c9‚Äîr5c3 (edges r3c6‚Äîr3c9 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr6c3), r5c9‚Äîr6c8 (edges r3c9‚Äîr8c9 ‚Üî r6c3‚Äîr6c6), r8c5‚Äîr9c6 (edges r8c9‚Äîr8c3 ‚Üî r6c6‚Äîr3c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r6c6=r7c7=r8c3], [r5c9=r9c8], r9c2, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (SET+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 3, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c6, r9c2} = {r3c9, r8c3}.
      ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r6c6, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c9, r8c3, r9c2, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r3c9, r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c9) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 9 {[r6c6=r9c8], r9c2, r9c9}: r9c2=X ‚Üí [r6c6=r9c8], r9c9‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r5c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c6=r9c8], r7c7, r9c9}: [r6c6=r9c8], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6, r8c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r7c7=X ‚Üí r7c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, [r6c6=r9c8], r7c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {[r6c6=r9c8], r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r6c6=r9c8], r9c2‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r5c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {[r6c6=r9c8], r7c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c7‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: r6c3, [r6c6=r9c8]‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c6=r9c8], r6c8}: r5c8=X and r6c8=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) ‚âÝ color(r9c8). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c9, r5c9, r5c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c9, r5c3} and {r5c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c9) = color(r5c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r6c3, r8c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c8=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c6, r9c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c2=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c6=X
              box 9 {r7c7, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c7=X
              r7c6=X and r7c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.

              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X

        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c6=r7c7], r6c3, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X

            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c3=r6c7], r6c3, r8c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c7]‚Äîr6c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.

      ‚Üí color(r8c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Guardian: [r5c3=r6c7] guards oddagon {[r5c3=r6c7], r5c9, r3c9, r3c6, [r6c6=r8c3]} (source [r8c9=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c3=r6c7]) = color([r8c9=r9c6]). Identify.
  5.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c7=r8c9=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c3=r6c7], r6c3, r8c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c7]‚Äîr6c3).
      ‚Üí color(r6c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c3=r6c7], [r6c6=r8c3], r8c7} (spine [r5c3=r6c7]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c3) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c3=r6c7], [r6c3=r8c7], [r6c6=r8c3], r8c5} (spine [r6c3=r8c7]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c3=r6c7]) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c3=r8c7], [r5c3=r6c7=r8c5], [r6c6=r8c3], r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r6c7=r8c5]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color([r6c3=r8c7]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c6, [r6c3=r8c7=r9c6], [r6c6=r8c3], [r5c3=r6c7=r8c5]} (spine [r6c3=r8c7=r9c6]‚Äî[r6c6=r8c3]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r5c3=r6c7=r8c5]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r5c3=r6c7=r8c5], r5c9, r5c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c3=r6c7=r8c5]‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r3c6=r5c3=r6c7=r8c5], [r3c9=r5c8], r5c9, r9c9} (spine [r3c9=r5c8]‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r5c3=r6c7=r8c5]) = color(r9c9). Identify.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r8c7:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {[r5c9=r8c7], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c3=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r8c7], r8c5‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r8c7], r9c9}: [r5c9=r8c7], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, [r5c9=r8c7]}: r5c3, [r5c9=r8c7]‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
              box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r8c7], r6c7}: r5c8=X and r6c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 8 {[r5c9=r8c7], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c5=X ‚Üí [r5c9=r8c7], r8c3‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r8c7], r9c9}: [r5c9=r8c7], r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              r6c6=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r6c3, r8c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r8c7], r6c7}: [r5c9=r8c7], r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c8=X
              r5c3=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) ‚âÝ color(r8c7). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, r8c7, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r8c7} and {r5c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r8c7) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c3, r8c5‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c7=X
              r6c7=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c7=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c7=X and r8c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c9=X ‚Üí r6c7‚âÝX
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c7‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+oddwheel) ===

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r8c3, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c3, r5c9, [r8c3=r9c9]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color([r8c3=r9c9]). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c7, r7c7, r8c8, r8c6} (source [r5c8=r8c3=r9c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color([r5c8=r8c3=r9c9]). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r5c8=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c6, r6c7, r7c7, r8c8, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r8c3) ‚âÝ color(r9c9). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r5c9, r6c7, r6c6, r8c6, r8c3, r9c9}
            Bridges: r5c8‚Äîr8c8 (edges r5c9‚Äîr6c7 ‚Üî r8c6‚Äîr8c3), r6c2‚Äîr9c2 (edges r6c7‚Äîr6c6 ‚Üî r8c3‚Äîr9c9), r3c6‚Äîr3c9 (edges r6c6‚Äîr8c6 ‚Üî r9c9‚Äîr5c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2, r6c6} (spine r6c1‚Äîr6c2).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, [r5c3=r6c6], r9c3, r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]‚Äîr9c3).

  3.  Diamond {r3c6, [r5c3=r6c6], [r8c3=r9c6], r9c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]‚Äî[r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c3=r6c6], [r3c6=r9c3], [r8c3=r9c6], r9c9} (spine [r3c6=r9c3]‚Äî[r8c3=r9c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c3=r6c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c3], [r5c3=r6c6=r9c9], [r8c3=r9c6]} (spine [r3c6=r9c3]‚Äî[r5c3=r6c6=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color([r8c3=r9c6]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c3], [r3c9=r8c3=r9c6], [r5c3=r6c6=r9c9], r5c9} (spine [r3c9=r8c3=r9c6]‚Äî[r5c3=r6c6=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c6=r9c3]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r8c3=r9c6], [r3c6=r5c9=r9c3], [r5c3=r6c6=r9c9], r5c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c9=r9c3]‚Äî[r5c3=r6c6=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color([r3c9=r8c3=r9c6]) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r8c3=r9c6], [r5c3=r6c6=r9c9], r7c8, r8c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c8, r9c6:
      Case A: color(r5c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, [r5c8=r9c6]}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r9c9} and {r3c9, [r5c8=r9c6]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {[r5c8=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí [r5c8=r9c6], r9c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c8=r9c6], r6c6}: r3c6=X ‚Üí r6c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r3c9, r5c9‚âÝX
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X ‚Üí r7c8, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c8=r9c6], r5c9}: [r5c8=r9c6], r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r5c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
              box 4 {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r6c1, r6c2‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c1, r6c2, r6c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color([r5c8=r9c6]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c8=r9c6], r5c9}: [r5c8=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r5c8=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: [r5c8=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r9c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c3=X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c8=r9c6], r6c6}: [r5c8=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              [r5c8=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c7‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) ‚âÝ color(r9c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c8, r9c6, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r9c6} and {r5c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c8, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X ‚Üí r3c6, r6c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              box 4 {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r6c1, r6c2‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c1, r6c2, r6c6}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r3c9=X
              r3c9=X ‚Üí r3c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r6c6=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8, r9c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r8c7=X
              r6c6=X ‚Üí r6c1, r6c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              box 4 {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2, r6c6} (spine r6c1‚Äîr6c2).
      ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c3=r6c6], r9c3, r9c6, r9c9} (spine r9c3‚Äîr9c6).
      ‚Üí color([r5c3=r6c6]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c6=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c8, r7c8, r8c7, r8c3, r9c3, r9c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c9, [r7c3=r9c9]} (spine r5c3‚Äîr5c9).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color([r7c3=r9c9]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c6=r7c3=r9c9], r6c6} (spine r3c6‚Äî[r5c6=r7c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], [r5c6=r7c3=r9c9], r5c9} (spine [r3c9=r6c6]‚Äî[r5c6=r7c3=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r3c6) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c6=r7c3=r9c9], [r3c6=r5c9], [r3c9=r6c6], r6c8} (spine [r3c6=r5c9]‚Äî[r3c9=r6c6]).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r7c3=r9c9]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  6.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r7c3=r9c9], r6c2, r8c2, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, r6c8, r6c2}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X ‚Üí r5c6, r5c9‚âÝX
        row 6 {r6c2, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c6‚âÝX

        r3c6=X ‚Üí r3c9‚âÝX (adjacent)

        col 2 {r6c2, r7c2, r8c2}: r6c2, r8c2‚âÝX ‚Üí r7c2=X

        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:

      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c8‚Üîr9c9 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.

          r5‚Üîr9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6) ‚Üí pairs differ.

      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6‚Äîr3c9, r5c6‚Äîr5c9, r9c6‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r5c3 (rung 2), r9c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
      row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}
      Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (box, box, box); row 5‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, col); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
      ‚Üí same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_parity] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r5c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r5c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r5c9], r5c3, r6c2, r6c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r5c9], r6c2} and {r5c3, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r5c9]) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {[r3c6=r5c9], r5c3, r5c6}: [r3c6=r5c9]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r5c9], r5c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r5c9]=X ‚Üí r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r3c6=r5c9], r3c9, r9c9}: [r3c6=r5c9]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r9c9‚âÝX
              r6c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c3) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {[r3c6=r5c9], r5c3, r5c6}: r5c3=X ‚Üí [r3c6=r5c9], r5c6‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c5‚âÝX
              col 6 {[r3c6=r5c9], r5c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r5c9], r5c6‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c6=X
              r5c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c8=X ‚Üí r8c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c6=X ‚Üí r8c5, r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) ‚âÝ color(r5c9). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, r5c9, r5c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr5c9).
            ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c3, r6c2, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c9=r5c6], r6c2} and {r5c3, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c9=r5c6]) = color(r6c2) = X
              row 5 {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c3, r5c9}: [r3c9=r5c6]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c2=X ‚Üí r6c5, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c6], r9c6}: [r3c9=r5c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6, r9c6‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r5c6]=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX
              r6c2=X ‚Üí r9c2‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c3, r5c9}: r5c3=X ‚Üí [r3c9=r5c6], r5c9‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c2, r6c8‚âÝX
              col 9 {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r5c6], r5c9‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c9=X
              r5c3=X ‚Üí r8c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r6c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c9=X ‚Üí r8c8, r9c2, r9c6‚âÝX (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

          r8‚Üîr9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7) ‚Üí pairs differ.
        4 distinct pairs, only 3 per Œ¥ class. Contradiction.
      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r5c9} and {r3c9, r5c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

=== Greedy proof (parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.
      Œ¥ ‚àà {‚àí1, +1}; 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
      row 6: r6c2, r6c5 (also r6c8)
      row 5: r5c3, r5c6 (also r5c9)
      row 9: r9c2, r9c6 (also r9c9)
      row 8: r8c3, r8c5 (also r8c7)
      Rows 5 and 6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4), r5c6‚Üîr6c5 (box 5), r5c9‚Üîr6c8 (box 6) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.
      Rows 5 and 9: r5c6‚Üîr9c6 (col 6), r5c9‚Üîr9c9 (col 9) ‚Äî 2 of 3 positions.
        Relative permutation is 3-cycle (same Œ¥) or transposition (opposite Œ¥, fixing r5c3 and r9c2).
      Case 1 (same Œ¥): row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 have same Œ¥. 4 rows; only 3 color pairs per Œ¥ value.
        row 5, row 6, row 8, row 9 pairwise distinct:
          r5‚Üîr6: r5c3‚Üîr6c2 (box 4) ‚Üí pairs differ.
          r5‚Üîr8: r5c3‚Üîr8c3 (col 3) ‚Üí pairs differ.

      Case 2 (opposite Œ¥): color(r5c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Gradient chain (with identification):
      For each row, Œ¥ = color(B) ‚àí color(A) mod 3, where A and B are the first and second listed cells.

      Rows 8 and 9: r8c3‚Üîr9c2 (box 7), r8c5‚Üîr9c6 (box 8), r8c7‚Üîr9c9 (box 9) ‚Äî derangement ‚Üí same Œ¥.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (ladder+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6‚Äîr3c9, r5c6‚Äîr5c9, r9c6‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r5c3 (rung 2), r9c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Parity transport:
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
      row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c8}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c9}
      Parallel links: row 5‚Üîrow 6 (box, box, box); row 5‚Üîrow 9 (box, col, col); row 8‚Üîrow 9 (box, box, box)
      ‚Üí same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column ‚Üí distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available ‚Üí pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_parity] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r5c6], r5c3, r6c2, r6c5}:

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

              r6c5=X ‚Üí r8c5‚âÝX (adjacent)

              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c6). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, r5c6, r9c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r9c6], r8c5, r8c3, r9c2}:

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c7}: r8c3=X ‚Üí r8c5, r8c7‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], r9c2, r9c9}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r9c2, r9c9‚âÝX
              col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], r5c6}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X ‚Üí r3c6, r5c6‚âÝX

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8, r9c9} (spine r7c8‚Äîr8c8).
      ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c8=r9c9], r9c3} (spine r5c3‚Äî[r5c8=r9c9]).
      ‚Üí color(r5c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r5c6=r9c3], r6c3, r6c5, r6c9} (spine r6c3‚Äîr6c5).
      ‚Üí color([r5c6=r9c3]) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c9=r9c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, [r5c8=r9c9], r9c4, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Branch on r5c6, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c6=r9c5], r5c8, r6c9, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c6=r9c5], r6c9} and {r5c8, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c6=r9c5]) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r9c5], r5c8}: [r5c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r5c3, r5c8‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c9=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c5‚âÝX
              row 9 {[r5c6=r9c5], r9c3, r9c9}: [r5c6=r9c5]=X ‚Üí r9c3, r9c9‚âÝX

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, [r5c6=r9c5], r5c8}: r5c8=X ‚Üí r5c3, [r5c6=r9c5]‚âÝX
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c5=X ‚Üí r6c3, r6c9‚âÝX
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3‚âÝX ‚Üí r9c3=X
              r5c8=X ‚Üí r7c8‚âÝX (adjacent)
              r9c3=X ‚Üí r9c9‚âÝX (adjacent)

              r8c7=X ‚Üí r8c6‚âÝX (adjacent)

              Contradiction.

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r6c3‚Äîr9c3, r6c5‚Äîr9c5, r6c9‚Äîr9c9}:
      Satellites r5c3 (rung 1), r3c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r3c9, r5c8:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8], r3c6, r5c6, r8c6} (spine r3c6‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        4.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r8c6], r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) ‚âÝ color(r5c8). Add edge.
        5.  Diamond {r5c3, r3c9, r5c8, r6c9} (spine r3c9‚Äîr5c8).
            ‚Üí color(r5c3) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        6.  Diamond {r5c8, r3c9, [r5c3=r6c9], r9c9} (spine r3c9‚Äî[r5c3=r6c9]).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        7.  Diamond {[r5c8=r9c9], [r5c3=r6c9], r5c6, r6c5} (spine [r5c3=r6c9]‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color([r5c8=r9c9]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        8.  K‚ÇÑ on {[r5c3=r6c9], [r5c8=r6c5=r9c9], r6c3, r9c3}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+SET+hexagon+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r3c6=r6c9], r5c6, r6c5} (spine [r3c6=r6c9]‚Äîr5c6).
            ‚Üí color(r5c8) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        3.  SET: {row 5, row 6}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{col 3, col 6}.
            Remainder: {[r3c6=r6c9], [r5c8=r6c5]} = {[r3c6=r6c9], r8c6, r9c3}.
            ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r6c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, [r5c8=r6c5], r6c3, r9c3, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c9). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r9c3, r5c3, r5c6}
            Bridges: r6c9‚Äîr6c3 (edges r3c6‚Äîr3c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr5c3), r6c9‚Äîr5c8 (edges r3c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r5c3‚Äîr5c6), r9c5‚Äîr8c6 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r5c6‚Äîr3c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

            ‚Üí Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r6c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, [r5c8=r6c5], r6c3, r9c3, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) ‚âÝ color(r6c9). Add edge.
        5.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r9c9, r9c3, r5c3, r5c6}
            Bridges: r6c9‚Äîr6c3 (edges r3c6‚Äîr3c9 ‚Üî r9c3‚Äîr5c3), r6c9‚Äîr5c8 (edges r3c9‚Äîr9c9 ‚Üî r5c3‚Äîr5c6), r9c4‚Äîr8c6 (edges r9c9‚Äîr9c3 ‚Üî r5c6‚Äîr3c6).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of C‚ÇÜ requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (SET+oddwheel+guardian) ===

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}‚Äâ‚àí‚Äâ{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      ‚Üí color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r4c9, r3c9, r3c6, r6c6} (source [r7c9=r8c6]).
      ‚Üí color(r6c8) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r4c9, r5c8, r9c8, r8c7, r8c3, r6c3, r6c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_hexagon] (diamond+xwing) ===

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r9c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

        col 9 {r3c9, [r4c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c9}: [r4c9=r8c3=r9c8]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r7c9‚âÝX

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c6) = X

        box 9 {[r4c9=r8c3=r9c8], r7c9, r8c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_hexagon] (diamond+SET+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r7c8} (spine r5c8‚Äîr6c8).
      ‚Üí color(r4c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.

      ‚Üí color(r9c3) = color([r4c9=r7c8]). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3], r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3]} and {r3c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r4c9=r7c8=r9c3]) = X
        row 9 {[r4c9=r7c8=r9c3], r9c6, r9c7}: [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r9c6, r9c7‚âÝX

        col 9 {r3c9, [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3], r8c9}: [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r3c9, r8c9‚âÝX
        [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3]=X ‚Üí r6c3‚âÝX (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

        col 9 {r3c9, [r4c9=r7c8=r9c3], r8c9}: r3c9=X ‚Üí r8c9‚âÝX

        box 9 {[r4c9=r7c8=r9c3], r8c9, r9c7}: all ‚âÝX, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. ‚ñ°
